# Design Your Project With a Partner You Can Trust.

Trust is worth its weight in gold, especially when a trusted partner can save you time, money and stress. Earning trust isn’t an easy thing to do – but we’re ready to show you how collaboration can result in a well-crafted design solution from a partner you can rely on again and again.

## WE BELIEVE IN

## Finding Good Ideas Everywhere.

We like to say that good ideas come from everywhere – especially unexpected places. Together, let’s create a roadmap that refines your project vision upfront, resulting in fresh ideas that are well executed – on task, on time and on budget.

## WE BELIEVE IN

## Access to Experienced Professionals Together  Under One Roof.

If you ask most of us how long we’ve been on team Warehaus, you’ll hear a lot of answers in the double digits. 10 years, 17 years, 25 years, 30+ years. There’s a lot of expertise under this roof – from architectural and interior design to civil and structural engineering. Our team brings unique viewpoints established through discipline and dedication to the craft.

## WE BELIEVE

## When Your Project is in Capable Hands, You Can Keep Your Focus Where It’s Needed.

One of our partners recently thanked us for providing a comprehensive response to a set of questions. Seems like a simple thing – but he said it was only one of a handful of times he got clear answers the first time –  in his 25 years of experience. The benefit – he didn’t have to run around to a variety of sources for answers. That’s what it means to Deliver.

## WE BELIEVE

## [Cookouts and Cornhole are Key to our Culture.](https://warehausae.com/culture/)

We’re serious about the work but don’t take ourselves too seriously. We genuinely enjoy each other’s company and know that team building ultimately makes us better partners for you.

## WE BELIEVE

## Trust Builds Relationships.

Trust means you believe in your partner’s ability to deliver. Trust means you can rest easy, knowing your project is in capable hands.

## Have a project in mind?

## Let’s talk.

## **We listen.** **We design.** **We deliver.**

# WAREHAUS

# about us

## Deep Roots. Rich History.

Warehaus is an award-winning team of designers with a 45-year legacy serving Central Pennsylvania and beyond. What began as a land surveying company has evolved to become a 40-person firm offering [architecture](http://warehausae.com/services/architecture/), [civil engineering and land planning](http://warehausae.com/services/civil_engineering/), [structural engineering](http://warehausae.com/services/structural/), [interior design](http://warehausae.com/services/interior_design/), [historic preservation](http://warehausae.com/work/historic/), and [building sciences](http://warehausae.com/work/building-sciences/) services.

Warehaus co-owners [Troy Bankert](http://warehausae.com/team/troy-bankert/) and [David Koratich](http://warehausae.com/team/david-koratich/) each have a long history at Warehaus, spanning as far back as 1988. Today, they oversee the day-to-day operations of the firm as well as overall asset management, guiding the strategic vision for ongoing growth and advancement.

![Troy Bankert and David Koratich, Warehaus Owners](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/Troy-Bankert-and-David-Koratich-2025-1-1024x683.jpg)

## Our History

### 1980

* * *

The Kinsley family of companies acquires Land Survey Consultants.

### 1980s-90s

* * *

The company, under various sub-entities, provides land survey consulting, civil and environmental services, architectural, interior and structural design to projects throughout the Mid-Atlantic region.

### 1998

* * *

All disciplines combine under the name LSC Design, Inc.

### 2017

* * *

![Warehaus Logo](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/Warehaus_Office_Painted_Signage-06_20-0011.jpg)

The company rebrands as Warehaus AE, inspired by a combination of the firm’s industrial roots and company culture.

### 2021

* * *

![Troy Bankert, CEO and David Koratich, COO, Warehaus Co-Owners](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/Dave_Troy-03_21.jpg)

Troy Bankert, AIA and David Koratich, EIT, two long-term employees, acquire independent ownership of Warehaus.

### 2022

* * *

![Warehaus Office - Architects, Engineers and Designers](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/06/Warehaus-Office-Aerial-e1725551198583.webp)

18 months after independent ownership, Warehaus purchases its office on North George Street in downtown York.

## Our Mission

We are Design Partners.We guide you through a collaborative experience with a common purpose, to deliver well-crafted solutions that benefit our clients, employees, shareholders, and communities.

## **We listen.** **We design.** **We deliver.**

# WAREHAUS

# civil & land planning

### Civil Engineering, Land Development, and Site Design

Since Warehaus was founded in 1980, our civil engineering team has partnered with public and private developers on a wide range of commercial, [industrial](http://warehausae.com/work/distribution_manufacturing/), [educational](http://warehausae.com/work/education/), and [residential](http://warehausae.com/work/residential-development/) projects. From simple estate work to large, complex commercial properties and residential developments, we specialize in land development and site design across York and Central Pennsylvania.

Our strong relationships with local governments throughout the Mid-Atlantic allow us to navigate approval processes efficiently. We ensure our client’s needs are met while meeting regulatory requirements quickly and effectively.

- Due Diligence Services
- Master Planning
- Land Use/Zoning Hearing Preperation
- Subdivision
- Land Development
- Stormwater and NPDES Permitting

- Utility Design, Permitting & Coordination
- Post Permitting Services (Construction Documents, Construction Administration Services, Post Construction & Record Plan Services)
- Municipal Engineering Services

![](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/Asset-1.svg)

## Civil Engineers Serving   Pennsylvania and Maryland

Warehaus’ [Civil Engineering Team](http://warehausae.com/team/dan-creep/) is experienced in townships, cities and boroughs across Pennsylvania and Maryland. In fact, we’ve worked in all 72 municipalities in York County. Whether it’s civil design, land development, or site planning, we have the local knowledge and expertise to deliver.

## **We listen.** **We design.** **We deliver.**

# WAREHAUS

# arts & culture

## An Enhanced Visitor Experience

Architecture is a celebration of art and culture. It is an opportunity to design, restore and renew places of significance and ignite a passion for the past. We’ve had the privilege to contribute to projects for a variety of inspiring places across the U.S.

- [Museums](http://warehausae.com/project/gettysburg-national-military-park-museum/)
- [Theaters](http://warehausae.com/project/capitol-theatre/)
- [Historic Landmarks](http://warehausae.com/project/george-spangler-farm/)

- [Gardens, State Parks & Conservatories](http://warehausae.com/project/sinnemahoning-state-park/)
- [Libraries](http://warehausae.com/project/kaltreider-benfer-library-york-county-library/)
- [Places of Religious Significance](http://warehausae.com/project/saint-john-paul-ii-national-shrine/)

## A Warehaus Partnership Brings

### Government Funding Experience

Throughout the history of our company, Warehaus has worked with clients, owners, and developers to navigate successfully through the requirements of government funding.

### Collaboration as a Key Value

In collaboration with national exhibit designers and world-renounced artisans and restorationists, Warehaus has designed arts and culture projects honoring our military, American industry and great figures in history – from [Milton Hershey](http://warehausae.com/project/milton-and-catherine-hershey-conservatory-at-hershey-gardens/) to [Saint John Paul II](http://warehausae.com/project/saint-john-paul-ii-national-shrine/).

### A Focus on Visitors and Patrons

Every design element works in concert to enhance each [visitor’s experience](http://warehausae.com/project/gettysburg-lincoln-railroad-station/) – inside and out.

### Relationship Driven Focus

We work hand-in-hand to guide our clients through an inclusive design process.

> “We greatly appreciate the contributions of the entire Warehaus team. They seamlessly blended creative planning with a commitment to preserve this historic space, to create an outstanding audience experience that matches the excellence of the entertainment stage and screen. The renovations greatly exceeded our expectations – the community loves the improvements and we’ve attracted many new patrons as well.”
>
> TODD FOGDALL
>
> APPELL CENTER FOR THE PERFORMING ARTS

## What our clients, partners, and community are saying about our work.

## **We listen.** **We design.** **We deliver.**

# WAREHAUS

# healthcare

## An Optimized Patient Experience

Your need to deliver the best healthcare relies on an efficient and safe environment, from the building design to the systems behind the scenes that keep it running. We understand the complexities and care required to create healthcare facilities – from [long-term master plans](http://warehausae.com/project/pshmc-crystala-offices/) to the design of labs, [operating rooms](http://warehausae.com/project/umd_fpi/) and [private waiting areas](http://warehausae.com/project/wellspan-health-thomas-hart-medical-office-building/), particularly in environments that need to remain safely operational during construction.

### Suites

- Cardiology
- Dermatology
- Endoscopy
- Hyperbarics
- Oncology
- Orthopedics
- Pediatrics
- Psychology
- Phlebotomy
- Shock Trauma
- Wound Care

### Buildings

- Acute Care Facilities
- Ambulatory Surgical Centers
- Eye & Ear Surgery Centers
- Medical Office Buildings
- Outpatient Medical Offices
- Private Physicians Imaging Centers
- Rehabilitation Facilities
- Women’s Health Centers

## A Warehaus Partnership Brings

### In-Depth Healthcare Experience

Including acute, [ambulatory](http://warehausae.com/project/umd_fpi/), outpatient and [physician’s office design](http://warehausae.com/project/wellspan-health-thomas-hart-medical-office-building/)

### A Focus on Patient Experience

[Driving our design](http://warehausae.com/project/acadia-healthcare-inc-bowling-green-brandywine/) – from where to park through check-in, extended stays and check-out

### Fluency in Healthcare Terminology

Gained through technical expertise in [workflow](http://warehausae.com/project/pshmc-crystala-offices/), [x-ray imaging](http://warehausae.com/project/back-2-health-chiropractic/) and [medical office design](http://warehausae.com/project/wellspan-health-thomas-hart-medical-office-building/)

### Speed-to-Market Efficiencies

That translate to effective coordination on key issues like schedule and budget

> “We were so fortunate for Warehaus to help us on our project. Erin and her team were so knowledgeable and walked us through the process every step of the way. They were very responsive, whether we called or emailed, and we were never left hanging wondering what was happening with our plans. We would recommend the team at Warehaus to anyone needing plan design for a new commercial space.”
>
> Dr. Jason Ellis
>
> Back 2 Chiropractic

# Dan Creep

Director of Engineering

Years in Industry: 19

Years with Warehaus: 19

Dan brings more than 18 years of experience to his role as Director of Engineering for Warehaus, where he is responsible for the operations of the civil engineering discipline. Dan delivers prompt, organized services and provides frequent progress reports that exemplify his attention to detail. He has extensive knowledge of many different construction phases, combined with a creative eye that gives him an edge in selecting unique designs for his clients.

With his project management experience, Dan has led a vast range of projects through entitlement from small single-family residential developments to large industrial parks totaling 2.4 million sf of warehouse space and is relied on to provide leadership to staff regarding constructability.

Dan holds a degree in Civil Engineering Technology from the University of Pittsburgh-Johnstown.

## SEND A MESSAGE

## Contact Dan

Have a project with civil engineering needs? Connect with Dan to discuss.

EMAIL

dcreep@warehausae.com

Phone

(717) 815-8503

###### WAREHAUS REFERRAL PROGRAM

## Help us Grow Our Civil Team

## LEARN MORE

###### What to know

## Referrals can be rewarding!

Know someone who would be a great fit for Warehaus’ Civil Designer role? You could receive $500\* for referring a contact to our open [Civil Designer role](http://warehausae.com/job/job-civil-designer/).

\\* The Fine Print:

_Your referred contact must apply for the Civil Designer role online. Referrals must be submitted prior to candidate application to be eligible._

_If your referred contact is hired for the Civil Designer role within 90 days of your referral submission, you will receive a $500 referral bonus, payable by check, provided the employee remains employed with Warehaus for at least 90 days._

_Employees of Warehaus are not eligible for this reward._

_To be eligible, all outlined steps must be followed. Payment will be issued after the referred employee completes 90 days of continuous employment._

_Warehaus reserves the right to modify or discontinue this program at any time._

## **We listen.** **We design.** **We deliver.**

# WAREHAUS

# historic preservation

## Preserving History

Our interdisciplinary team takes a balanced approach to historic preservation, seeking to highlight and preserve the historic resource while also considering the modern needs of the owner. Additionally, our long-term relationship with a highly regarded historic preservationist, coupled with our collective expertise in [architecture](http://warehausae.com/services/architecture) and [structural engineering](http://warehausae.com/services/structural), allows us to navigate projects with diverse funding stacks, including RACP, historic tax credits, new market tax credits and grant funding.

- Historic Preservation Design Consultation
- Determinations of Eligibility and National Register
- Preservation Regulation Services

- Historic Resource Surveys
- Historic Structures Reports and Preservation Plans

## Restoring the Spirit of Gettysburg.

Historic Preservation projects have taken us all over Pennsylvania and Maryland, but our work at [Gettysburg](http://warehausae.com/project/george-spangler-farm/) truly highlights the importance of preserving history at one of America’s most significant landmarks.

## **We listen.** **We design.** **We deliver.**

# WAREHAUS

# adaptive reuse

## Creative and Technical Expertise Combine to Transform Your Space

For a space you can count on for decades to come, you need a partner with the vision to transform and the technical expertise to ensure it lasts. Warehaus combines creative problem-solving with our knowledge of building materials and proper applications for varied building construction types to ensure you receive a space that is high-performing in all areas.

- [Facade Modifications](http://warehausae.com/project/umfp-redwood-facade-restoration/)
- [Rehabilitation](http://warehausae.com/project/1895-federal-building/)
- [Renovation Fit-Outs](http://warehausae.com/project/320-north-george-street-office/)
- [Restoration](http://warehausae.com/case-study-bischoff-inn/)

- [Historic Preservation](http://warehausae.com/project/george-spangler-farm/)
- [Building Materials](http://warehausae.com/project/hershey-historic-community-building/)
- [Envelope Repair](http://warehausae.com/project/carlisle-barracks-restoration/)

## A Warehaus Partnership Brings

### Investment in Technology

Our resources enable us to identify the causes of building damage, such as water infiltration, vapor condensation, thermal breach, physical building movement, lack of maintenance damage, [environmental impacts](http://warehausae.com/project/gratz-bank-tower-repair/) such as wind and water erosion, and structure overloading.

### Building Science Expertise

We have extensive experience with rehabilitation, renovation and restoration of [aging structures](http://warehausae.com/work__trashed/building-sciences/).

### Relationship Driven Collaboration

We draw on a supporting network of high quality specialists from all areas of the industry, from masonry restorationists, [envelope specialists](http://warehausae.com/work__trashed/building-sciences/), concrete manufacturers, acoustics, and [structural](http://warehausae.com/services/structural/), mechanical, plumbing, [civil](http://warehausae.com/services/civil_engineering/), electrical and lighting engineers.

### A Multi-Disciplinary Team

With [architects](http://warehausae.com/services/architecture/) and [engineers](http://warehausae.com/services/civil_engineering/) under one roof, our comprehensive team has the skills needed to provide streamlined building evaluations and programming assessments. One team that can deliver in one site visit.

> “Another successful adaptive reuse of the historic urban “fabric” – which is so important to the character of these towns and cities in which these projects are located. Aligning the entry, the auditorium, and the open work spaces underneath the taller roof, and using the existing clerestory windows to bring natural light into the space were good design decisions that made the most of the existing building.”
>
> AIA Central Pennsylvania Citation Award 2013
>
> Jury Comments: 320 North George Street Office

## What our clients, partners, and community are saying about our work.

## **We listen.** **We design.** **We deliver.**

# WAREHAUS

# structural engineering

## A Partner in Structural Integrity

Our Structural Engineering Team is integral to the project team, collaborating closely with [architects](http://warehausae.com/services/architecture/), [designers](http://warehausae.com/services/interior_design/), and [civil engineers](http://warehausae.com/services/civil_engineering/) from the start and during all phases of a project. Our scope of structural services includes design, coordination, inspection, rehabilitation, full documentation, and existing conditions evaluations. We can fully model structural design iterations, offering teams a complete picture of design options, cost implications, and project coordination tools from concept design through realization.

### Leading-Edge Technology

A leader in Building Information Modeling (BIM), Warehaus believes in ongoing training with the most current software platforms available in the industry

- Autodesk
- Revit
- Navisworks
- Risa Suite of Design Software
- RAM Steel/Structure
- NewForma Project Management

## An Industrial Niche

Our portfolio of industrial clients runs deep, and our [structural engineers](http://warehausae.com/team/derek-donnelly-pe/) have made contributions to hundreds of warehouse and distribution projects

## **We listen.** **We design.** **We deliver.**

# WAREHAUS

# corporate office

## A Reflection of Workplace Culture and Creativity

From [open concept environments](http://warehausae.com/project/320-north-george-street-office/) to [configurable spaces](http://warehausae.com/project/bartels-center/), we apply our rigorous, research-based Design Delivery Process to set the foundation for our clients’ programming needs.

- Sound Mitigation
- Integration of Audio Visual Technology
- Workspace Programming

- Furniture Selection
- Feasibility Studies
- Site Wayfinding

## A Warehaus Partnership Brings

### Design Best Practices

We believe the key is understanding your unique business needs and solving for those needs with research-backed strategies. The most successful spaces accommodate varying work styles, with [breakout spaces](http://warehausae.com/project/paypal-toronto-flagship-office/) for collaboration, nooks for privacy and focus, co-working areas, and convertible stations that accommodate visitors and hybrid workers.

### Clear Communication

At project kick-off, we present a communication plan to ensure every participant understands the flow of information. We review roles and identify decision makers and information sharing strategies to ensure that everyone is on the same page.

### Collaboration as a Key Value

Managing our relationships with integrity is of utmost importance to us. We have built trusting, long-lasting relationships with many subconsultants that add value to office designs, from acoustic consultants to food service designers.

### A Forward-Thinking Approach to Post-Covid Office Design

We’re on the leading edge of modern, [post-covid](http://warehausae.com/project/pshmc-crystala-offices/) workspace needs that foster collaboration and support remote and hybrid workers.

> “Warehaus offered artistic advice and suggestions under demanding circumstances with multiple changes to layouts, furniture, and lighting. They met the needs of this office to develop an approved design with utmost professionalism. They had the foresight during the course of the project to make a fast and solid recommendation based on experience and logic.”
>
> Leigh Goldman
>
> PayPal Construction Manager

## What our clients, partners, and community are saying about our work.

## **We listen.** **We design.** **We deliver.**

# WAREHAUS

# interior design

## Commercial Interior Design Solutions

Our [architectural](http://warehausae.com/services/architecture/) pre-design research process uncovers deeper wants and needs that result in human-centered interior design solutions, not just reflections of current trends.

The result can serve as a catalyst for inspiring ideas, a reflection of a corporate brand, or a tool to reinforce recruitment.

From programming to graphics to furniture design to technology integration, Warehaus has all of your interior design needs covered.

- Programming
- Graphics and Signage
- Acoustics
- Audio/Visual Components

- Lighting
- Artwork
- Furniture Specification and Design

## Why Work with an Interior Designer?

Informed Space Plans that Work

Programming that improves efficiency and functionality – like our work at [PayPal](http://warehausae.com/project/paypal-toronto-flagship-office/)

Optimized Interior Environments

Considering access to daylight, nature, views, and temperature control and task lighting – like our work at [Phoenix Contact](http://warehausae.com/project/phoenix-contact-office/)

Tailored Designs for Unique Work Styles

Furniture plans that enhance the work experience – like our work at [WellSpan Health](http://warehausae.com/project/bartels-center/)

Product Knowledge and Access to Resources

To recommend the most innovative solutions – like our work at [Holderness School](http://warehausae.com/project/holderness-school-davis-center/)

# Franklin Square Multi-Family Mixed Use

## New Freedom, PA

![Drone photo of Franklin Square mixed-use apartments and brewery](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/Franklin-Square-19-700x700.webp)

![Residential units at Franklin Square including townhomes and garden style apartments in New Freedom](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/Render-View-2-High-Res-1.jpg)

![Franklin Square Mixed-Use development with townhomes and apartments in New Freedom, PA](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/Render-View-1-High-Res-1-700x700.jpg)

![Luxury lofts above commercial spaces at Franklin Square in New Freedom](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/Render-View-3-1.jpg)

![Drone photo of Franklin Square mixed-use apartments and brewery](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/Franklin-Square-5-700x700.webp)

![Drone photo of Franklin Square mixed-use apartments and brewery](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/Franklin-Square-8-700x700.webp)

## Mixed-use, multi-family project coming to New Freedom

Located in New Freedom, PA, the Franklin Square Mixed-Use development offers quick access to I-83. Both townhome and apartment residents can enjoy the spacious, wide-open farmland with easy commutes to York, Pennsylvania and Baltimore, Maryland.

Project Details

Client

Burkentine Builders

Size

Two 3-story mixed-use buildings

Completion Date

In Process - 2023

Services

[Architecture](https://warehausae.com/service/architecture/)

Industries

[Multi-Family](https://warehausae.com/industry/multi-family/)

![Residential units at Franklin Square including townhomes and garden style apartments in New Freedom](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/Render-View-2-High-Res-1.jpg)

### We listen.

Franklin Square was designed in three phases, with townhomes built as part of Phase I, and apartments built as part of Phase II.

### We design.

Warehaus partnered with Burkentine Builders on Phase III, to create schematic designs for two 3-story mixed-use buildings at the southwest entrance to the Franklin Square community, along Cambridge Boulevard. The community is a grand total of 154 residential units including townhomes, garden style apartments, in addition to twenty new luxury lofts to be built above the commercial spaces which are adjacent to the Franklin Square 3-story townhome and 1-3 bedrooms apartment community.

![Franklin Square Mixed-Use development with townhomes and apartments in New Freedom, PA](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/Render-View-1-High-Res-1.jpg)

![Drone photo of Franklin Square mixed-use apartments and brewery](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/Franklin-Square-FEATURE-768x400.webp)

### We deliver.

Burkentine Station features state of the art amenities including rooftop gardens and a new craft brewery where Franklin Square residents, the community, Rail Trail enthusiasts and tourists to Historic New Freedom will enjoy craft beer and casual dining with both indoor and outdoor options.

# 320 North George Street Office

## York, PA

## “A great example of adaptive reuse…an exemplary use of an industrial palette.” - Central PA AIA

The renovation at 320 North George Street transformed a 45,000 square-foot, turn-of-the century warehouse into a sustainable, LEED Platinum certified office of the 21st century – leading the way for modern design that respects the history and tradition of Downtown York.

Project Details

Client

Warehaus

Size

45,000 sf

Completion Date

2013

Services

[Architecture](https://warehausae.com/service/architecture/), [Civil](https://warehausae.com/service/civil/), [Historic](https://warehausae.com/service/historic/), [Interiors](https://warehausae.com/service/interiors/)

Industries

[Adaptive Reuse](https://warehausae.com/industry/adaptive-reuse/), [Historic](https://warehausae.com/industry/historic/), [Office](https://warehausae.com/industry/office/)

![North facing exterior of the adaptive reuse project at 320 North George Street](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/Warehaus-Headquarters-05_2014-0574.jpg)

### We listen.

Through an adaptive reuse effort, the industrial beauty of the original structure is celebrated inside and out.

### We design.

Exterior modifications returned the main façade to its original pedestrian-focused use, with leasable street front space for merchants. Industrial elements, like an overhead crane, are preserved in place as a relic of the building’s original use. The expansive interior of the warehouse highlights the character of exposed brick and steel and the warmth of natural light. Warehaus’ highly collaborative work environment incorporates work stations, team spaces and conference rooms with both modern and classic styling – a juxtaposition of old and new.

![Collaborative work environment with work stations, team spaces and conference rooms with modern and classic styling](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/Warehaus-Headquarters-05_2014-0551.jpg)

![Natural light streaming into the interior highlighting the character of the building in this adaptive reuse project](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/Warehaus-Headquarters-05_2014-0443-1.jpg)

### We deliver.

The project achieved LEED Platinum certification in October of 2014 and was awarded Innovative Project of the Year by Central PA’s USGBC at their 2014 Forever Green Awards. The renovation was also recognized by Central PA’s AIA chapter with an Honor Award, calling the project, “a great example of adaptive re-use, incorporating factory elements into the new design, and an exemplary use of an industrial palette.”

## **We listen.** **We design.** **We deliver.**

# WAREHAUS

# culture

## Welcome to  Warehaus

Our dynamic team is driven by hard work and a passion for what we do. But we also believe in fostering a collaborative culture that embraces fun and camaraderie. We know that a strong team is built on more than just work assignments. That’s why we make it a priority to hang out and have a great time together. We believe in building meaningful connections, both within the office and beyond.

![give-local-2025-05022025-10](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/Give-Local-2025-05022025-10-scaled.jpg)

## Our Values

### Conscience

I am accountable for my actions.

### Conscience

I contribute to Warehaus’ legacy with integrity, by upholding our vision and mission. I can be trusted to do what I say I will do – thoroughly, accurately and on time.

### Collaboration

I am a partner.

### Collaboration

I value individual strengths and strive for team success. I communicate clearly and make it a priority to respect my colleagues’, clients’ and communities’ ideas and needs.

### Curiosity

I seek out knowledge so I can grow.

### Curiosity

I actively listen, ask questions, am open to change and adapt as needed. I strive to improve, and I find solutions when faced with challenges.

### Conservation

I respect time, energy and resources.

### Conscience

In the interest of Warehaus and our clients, I follow a budget, stay informed on how my actions impact our financials, and take action to ensure efficiency and cost effectiveness.

![](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/0c8ca3fe-4ae4-4ae4-bca5-6881fb64436d-700x600.jpg)

[**cornhole**](https://warehausae.com/culture/#)

![](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/Dave-Dustin-Grill-1-700x700.webp)

[**cookouts**](https://warehausae.com/culture/#)

![](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/2022_03_15_0120-1-700x700.webp)

[**celebrations**](https://warehausae.com/culture/#)

![](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/IMG_8373-700x700.jpg)

[**community**](https://warehausae.com/culture/#)

![](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/Civil-Mini-Golf-Activity-July-2023-3-700x700.webp)

[**camaraderie**](https://warehausae.com/culture/#)

## Haus Perks

We value and appreciate our team members and have a range of employee engagement initiatives in place to show our gratitude for their hard work and dedication. From recognition programs to Haus parties, we make sure every team member feels valued and supported.

## **We listen.** **We design.** **We deliver.**

# WAREHAUS

# education

## Enriched Educational Experiences

The classroom is more than just a space for learning. It’s the place where experiences unfold and ignite lifelong passions for writing, drama, music, science and more. What fuels our passion is designing spaces where those experiences impact the learner, and that requires more than showstoping aesthetics. It requires a team willing to dig deep to discover solutions that enrich living, working and learning.

- Understanding of Various Educational Methodologies
- Furniture and Finish Selection

- Acoustics and Lighting Systems
- Technology Integration

## A Warehaus Partnership Brings

### An Investment in Thought Leadership

Warehaus values and contributes to thought leadership in the academic arena to ensure we are always progressing in the mission to design [21st-century learning environments](http://warehausae.com/project/holderness-school-davis-center/). Our passion for [educational design](http://warehausae.com/project/york-country-day-school/) drives us to continually seek knowledge to support our education clients.

### An Informed Toolkit for Design

We design from the inside out. Before pencil ever goes to paper, we work with you to develop a rich program that dives deeper than just ceilings, floors, walls and square footage. Together, we build a toolkit that takes into account all space parameters, to ensure you have all the tools necessary to deliver enriched [educational experiences](http://warehausae.com/project/york-academy-regional-charter-school-upper-school/) within your walls and beyond.

### A Commitment to Sustainability

We’ve completed more than 20 LEED projects for elementary schools, [colleges](http://warehausae.com/project/york-college-of-pennsylvania-kinsley-engineering-center/), [offices](http://warehausae.com/project/320-north-george-street-office/) and [corporate environments](http://warehausae.com/project/pandgtablerstation/). These projects exemplify Warehaus’ commitment to sustainable design principles, which we apply as an integral part of our services.

### Building Science Knowledge

Our expertise in building materials and systems and construction types creates high-performing buildings you can count on for decades.

> “Warehaus pushed us to answer tough design questions, like “How does Holderness’ setting inform science education?” Leading by questions is a signature part of the way the Warehaus team works.”
>
> Phil Peck
>
> Holderness School

## What our clients, partners, and community are saying about our work.

## **We listen.** **We design.** **We deliver.**

# WAREHAUS

# building sciences

## An Exacting Approach to Repair and Restoration

We are often called on by some of the best masonry contractors and consultants in the Mid-Atlantic to conduct [envelope repair](http://warehausae.com/project/hershey-historic-community-building/) and [building need assessments](http://warehausae.com/project/st-paul-evangelical-lutheran-church/) for both local and national clients. It’s our expertise in conducting and documenting need assessments and physical conditions for various building types that gives us the know-how to do the job right while saving our clients both time and money.

- Historic Preservation
- Building Materials

- Envelope Repair
- Facade Modifications

## A Warehaus Partnership Brings

### Experienced Partners

We draw on a supporting network of high-quality specialists from all areas of the industry, from masonry restorationists, concrete manufacturers, acoustics, and [structural](http://warehausae.com/services/structural/), mechanical, plumbing, [civil](http://warehausae.com/services/civil_engineering/), electrical and lighting engineers.

### Investment in Technology

Our resources enable us to identify the causes of building damage, such as [water infiltration](http://warehausae.com/project/umfp-redwood-facade-restoration/), vapor condensation, thermal breach, physical building movement, lack of maintenance damage, [environmental impacts](http://warehausae.com/project/gratz-bank-tower-repair/) such as wind and water erosion, and structure overloading.

### Continuing Education

As technology advances, we continue to pursue ongoing education in the evolution of building products and treatments.

### Clear Communication

Our process ensures we maintain close contact with contractors – for approval of means and methods for proper application – to ensure a high level of workmanship during construction.

> "The team at Warehaus excels in its attention to details, commitment to a unique design intent that meets the client’s needs, and their thoroughness to achieve a quality end plan."
>
> ROGER DERR
>
> WITMER RESTORATION, INC.

## **We listen.** **We design.** **We deliver.**

# WAREHAUS

# multi-family

## Vibrant Living Spaces from Coast to Coast

We examine every facet of an environment, contributing to strong communities and providing safe and secure homes and prosperous businesses. We’ve utilized this process to design vibrant living spaces – like [apartments](http://warehausae.com/project/nelson-kohl-apartments/) and [multi-family](http://warehausae.com/project/franklin-square-multi-family-mixed-use/), [mixed-use projects](http://warehausae.com/project/the-steel-works-mixed-use-development/) – that incorporate sought-after amenities and appealing aesthetics to attract discerning residents.

- Design Advisory
- Material Selection
- Decorative Lighting

- Space Planning
- Custom Furniture

## A Warehaus Partnership Brings

### A 40+ Year Legacy

Our 40+ years of experience informs our innovative, award-winning designs. Contextual storytelling will make your project [a destination](http://warehausae.com/project/nelson-kohl-apartments/).

### Creative Approach to Site Adaptation

Your project site’s geography, climate and context is unique. We thoughtfully [adapt designs to your site](http://warehausae.com/project/800-south-queen-street-multi-family-apartments/) and mitigate regulatory challenges so that your project kicks off successfully.

### Relationship Driven Focus

We work hand-in-hand to guide our clients through an inclusive design process.

### Speed to Market Efficiencies

Our tested and proven speed to market efficiencies translate to effective coordination on key issues like schedule and budget.

> The Nelson Kohl Apartments project is transformational. Its exterior is a conceptual canvas, bringing color and movement to a once desolate lot, while being informed by the existing neighborhood. The simple form is sensitively crafted and boldly unconventional, and the Nelson Kohl Apartments is a model for projects that aspire to civic engagement and discernible impact.
>
> AIA Central Pennsylvania Merit Award 2019
>
> Jury Comments: Nelson Kohl Apartments

## **We listen.** **We design.** **We deliver.**

# WAREHAUS

# historic ARCHITECTURE

## Respect for Historic Character

We are drawn to historic buildings because of what they can tell us about ourselves – about the history of our cultures and our communities. Our long-term relationship with a highly regarded historic preservationist, coupled with our collective expertise in [architecture](http://warehausae.com/services/architecture/) and [structural engineering](http://warehausae.com/services/structural/), ensures we respect a place’s historic character and significance while preparing it to function into its next century.

- [Building Science is our forte](http://warehausae.com/work__trashed/building-sciences/)
- We have strong relationships with local masonry contractors and historic preservationists
- Collaboration is enhanced with architectural and structural resources under one roof

## A Warehaus Partnership Brings

### Knowledgeable Resources

Our long-term relationship with a highly regarded historic preservationist enables our team to hit the ground running on [historic preservation projects](http://warehausae.com/case-study-bischoff-inn/).

### Government Funding Experience

Throughout the history of our company, Warehaus has worked with clients, owners, and developers to navigate successfully through the requirements of [government funding.](http://warehausae.com/case-study-bischoff-inn/)

### A Diverse Project Portfolio

Our consultation on a diverse array of projects, ranging from landscapes and [battlefields](http://warehausae.com/project/little-round-top/) to [farms](http://warehausae.com/project/george-spangler-farm/) and [apartment buildings](http://warehausae.com/project/205-state-street-apartments/), gives us the experience to confirm that all aspects of a project’s design are compatible with the historic character of the property and meet the Secretary of the Interior’s Standards for the Treatment of Historic Properties.

### Thorough Research

Detailed analysis and documentation of individual properties and historic districts is performed for clients seeking Determinations of Eligibility and/ or nomination to the National Register of Historic Places, which makes them eligible for relevant [grants](http://warehausae.com/project/lancaster-county-courthouse-and-prison/), protective easements and [historic tax credits](http://warehausae.com/project/1895-federal-building/).

> “The George Spangler Farm transformation began in 2008 when the Gettysburg Foundation acquired this historic 80-acre property. Since then, we’ve embarked on a once-in-a-lifetime journey of discovery and preservation alongside our incredible partners, Warehaus and Kinsley Construction. The hard work, craftsmanship, and commitment to the preservation and education of our nation’s history is inspiring. We’re proud they received this well-deserved recognition – the 2020 Pennsylvania Historic Preservation Award Winner for Restoration - and are honored to be a part of it.”
>
> Elle Lamboy
>
> Gettysburg Foundation Vice President for Philanthropy

# WAREHAUS

architecture

## Partner with an Architect  that Listens, Designs, and Delivers.

No one knows your business better than you. It’s for that rea­son we believe collaboration is the key to mutual success.

To­gether, we’ve created a roadmap that refines the project vision upfront, resulting in fresh ideas that articulate a clear design intention from the very start.

Whether your project is a [Design Build](http://warehausae.com/project/york-country-day-school/) in York, a [test fit](http://warehausae.com/project/test-fits/) in Lancaster, or a [renovation](http://warehausae.com/project/york-country-day-school/) in Harrisburg, you’ll find a trustworthy partner in the team at Warehaus.

- Architecture

- Interior Design


Interior Design
- Master Planning

- Sustainable Design

- Specification Writing

- Business Information Modeling

- Historic Preservation

- Code & Accessibility Consulting

- Building Sciences


## Our Architectural Approach

We like to say that good ideas come from everywhere – especially unexpected plac­es. By investing in a robust upfront discovery process, we’ve built a well-rounded approach to problem-solving.

Comprehensive Research

We gain valuable insight by exploring successful solutions implemented in surprising places around the world. Precedent images help to illustrate intentions.

Community Feedback

By gathering feedback from community members early in the ideation process, we incorporate the wants and needs of residents, business owners, and invested community members into our proposed solution.

Design Charettes

Design charettes fuel the idea­tion process by bringing together all invested parties and exploring together the question of “What if?”

Concept Sketches

Through the incorporation of first-hand experience, community feedback, research, and client needs, all the details come together in concept sketches – they are the first step in bringing the vision to life!

# Hershey Community Building

## Hershey, PA

![Terracotta tile roof restoration at Hershey Community Building restoration](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/DSC02854.jpg)

![Terracotta tile roof restoration at Hershey Community Building restoration](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/DSC02873.jpg)

![Restoration of windows, doors and skylights at Hershey Community Building](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/DSC02834.jpg)

![Historic Photo of the Hershey Community Building from the Hershey Community Archives](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/14-East-Chocolate-Avenue-The-Hershey-Community-Building-05_19-0011.jpg)

![Hershey Community Building Sciences project building repair](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/14-East-After-700x700.jpg)

![Restoration of windows, doors and skylights at Hershey Community Building](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/DSC02910.jpg)

![Restoration of windows, doors and skylights at Hershey Community Building](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/DSC02831.jpg)

![Terracotta tile roof restoration at Hershey Community Building restoration](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/DSC02854.jpg)

![Terracotta tile roof restoration at Hershey Community Building restoration](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/DSC02873.jpg)

![Restoration of windows, doors and skylights at Hershey Community Building](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/DSC02834.jpg)

![Historic Photo of the Hershey Community Building from the Hershey Community Archives](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/14-East-Chocolate-Avenue-The-Hershey-Community-Building-05_19-0011.jpg)

![Hershey Community Building Sciences project building repair](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/14-East-After-700x700.jpg)

![Restoration of windows, doors and skylights at Hershey Community Building](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/DSC02910.jpg)

![Restoration of windows, doors and skylights at Hershey Community Building](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/DSC02831.jpg)

## Restoring a historic Hershey landmark to it's previous glory

During the Great Depression, Milton S. Hershey embarked on his “Great Building Campaign” to bolster the local economy which included the historic Hershey Community Building, located at 14 East Chocolate Avenue and completed in 1933.

Project Details

Client

The Hershey Company

Size

N/A - Facade restoration

Completion Date

2018

Services

[Architecture](https://warehausae.com/service/architecture/), [Historic](https://warehausae.com/service/historic/)

Industries

[Building Sciences](https://warehausae.com/industry/building-sciences/), [Historic](https://warehausae.com/industry/historic/)

![Historic Photo of the Hershey Community Building from the Hershey Community Archives](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/14-East-Chocolate-Avenue-The-Hershey-Community-Building-05_19-0011.jpg)

### We listen.

Warehaus was selected to manage the owner’s stewardship of the downtown landmark, providing analysis and documentation necessary to restore the stone building’s many beautiful features.

### We design.

The project began with a complete 3D scan of the existing building, recording the high level of architectural detail and allowing the project team to create a Building Information Model (BIM). Warehaus’ expertise in a variety of building materials, including granite, limestone, brick, and terracotta, aided in the analysis required for the restoration. Additional materials used in the detailed facade included wood, steel, brass, bronze, and wrought iron – each requiring their own individual restoration solutions. The project also involved the restoration and replacement of 500+ windows, doors and skylights that included delicate treatment of stain glass windows.

![Restoration of windows, doors and skylights at Hershey Community Building](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/DSC02831.jpg)

![Hershey Community Building Sciences project building repair](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/14-East-After.jpg)

### We deliver.

The team used custom solutions and specialized tools such as video scoping to identify problem areas within the internal downspout system in order to properly manage the water infiltration experienced within the building. Warehaus also coordinated the historically accurate terracotta tile roof along with replicating the highly detailed copper flashings. Warehaus’ internal Historic Preservation team was instrumental in making certain rehabilitation plans met the exacting standards required for a 20% historic tax credit.

# Comprehensive Civil Engineering Services Tailored for Municipalities

Our proficiency with a wide array of disciplines and municipal regulations, laws and permitting makes Warehaus an excellent choice to represent your municipality. We bring expertise, in-depth knowledge of the regulatory environment, and the skills necessary to address complex infrastructure, environmental and land use issues.

## Project Info

PROJECT LOCATION

Throughout York County

Owner/Client

Engineer of Record

for New Freedom Borough

Project Type

Civil Engineering

Completion Date

Ongoing

### Engineer of Record

In 2021, Warehaus was selected as the Municipal Engineer for New Freedom Borough, to assist with review of subdivision and land development plans, review text amendments and zoning related items filed by applicants and provide construction oversight for new developments.

![Dan Creep and Zach Michali of Warehaus and Andrew Shaffer of New Freedom Borough](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/12/Rail-Trail-Improvements-700x700.webp)

Dan Creep and Zach Michali of Warehaus and Andrew Shaffer of New Freedom Borough

### Rail Trail Improvements

Warehaus led a project to enhance the Rail Trail crosswalks at Main Street and Franklin Street. The aim was to revitalize the downtown area, focusing on aesthetic and safety improvements. Stamped thermoplastic crosswalks were introduced to enhance the pedestrian pathway visually, while decorative poles were installed to house signage, improving the area’s appearance. Safety enhancements included the installation of Rectangular Rapid Flashing Beacons (RRFBs) triggered by infrared bollards, alerting motorists to pedestrian presence. Warehaus provided various services, including securing approvals, preparing construction plans, managing the bid process, and conducting construction inspections to ensure project success.

![New Freedom Borough rail Trail Improvements](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/12/Rail-Trail-Improvements-8-700x700.webp)

New Freedom Borough rail Trail Improvements

![New Freedom Borough Rail Trail](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/12/Rail-Trail-Improvements-6-700x700.webp)

New Freedom Borough Rail Trail

![New Freedom Borough Rail Trail Improvements](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/12/Rail-Trail-Improvements-4-700x700.webp)

New Freedom Borough Rail Trail Improvements

![New Freedom Borough rail Trail Improvements](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/12/Rail-Trail-Improvements-11-700x700.webp)

New Freedom Borough rail Trail Improvements

### Paving Project

Warehaus’ collaborated with New Freedom Borough staff to identify and prioritize roads in need of repair. Warehaus managed the public bid process for the 2023 Paving Project, aimed at upgrading the borough’s road infrastructure. The project included infill and overlay work, full-depth shoulder reconstruction, and base repairs along a total length of 2,713 linear feet of roads. Warehaus handled the preparation, submission, and management of the public bid process, including creating construction drawings outlining the project’s scope and work items. They facilitated the selection of the contractor and remained actively engaged in daily inspections to ensure the successful execution and completion of the project, demonstrating a commitment to procedural compliance.

![](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/New-Freedom-Borough-Paving-Project-700x537.webp)

### Sewage Enforcement Officer

Stephen Stone, EIT
Sewage Enforcement Officer
SEO# 04183

Warehaus offers SEO capabilities through Stephen Stone, EIT, a valued member of the Warehaus team.

Stephen brings extensive civil engineering expertise and a strong commitment to public health, complementing Warehaus’s 45 years of experience delivering civil engineering solutions across Central Pennsylvania..

With Stephen’s dedication and availability, Warehaus is well-equipped to support sewage management needs while upholding its reputation for excellence in serving local communities.

![Stephen Stone, Sewage Enforcement Officer](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/Stone-Stephen.webp)

Stephen Stone, Sewage Enforcement Officer

## **We listen.** **We design.** **We deliver.**

# WAREHAUS

# RESIDENTIAL DEVELOPMENT

## Planning our Communities

In both rural and urban environments, Warehaus is adept at [land planning](http://warehausae.com/services/civil_engineering/) and development for many types of residential projects – including [multi-family](http://warehausae.com/project/donwood-estates/) and [single-family sites](http://warehausae.com/project/single-family-lots-civil-engineering/) – including garden-style units, townhomes, and high-rise construction.

- Site Development and Grading Plans
- Subdivision
- Stormwater Management
- Utility Coordination

- Geotechnical Investigations
- Erosion and Sediment Control Plans
- Zoning and Municipal Ordinance Approvals
- Coordination with PennDOT for State Road Access

## A Warehaus Partnership Brings

### A Focus on Your Needs

We listen carefully to our clients’ needs and navigate through regulatory requirements to ensure those needs are met efficiently and effectively.

### Municipal Experience

We work with our clients to ensure their vision of development aligns with municipal land use and zoning regulations. Our team of experts provides detailed guidance to navigate through the necessary municipal approval process.

### Strong Relationships

Our strong relationships with local government throughout the Mid-Atlantic enables us to understand and streamline approval processes.

### Sub-Division Expertise

When a client’s vision requires creation or adjustment of property lines, we have the expertise to manage the land development process, including infrastructure design such as roadways, utilities and stormwater management.

# WellSpan Health York Hospital Expansion

## York, PA

![WellSpan York Hospital](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/07/Wellspan_York_Hospital_Pedestrian_Bridge.webp)

![Healthcare design for medical facilities](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/IMG_5584-700x700.jpg)

![Demolition of older buildings on York Hospital campus to make way for expansion](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/IMG_5546-700x700.jpg)

![Groundbreaking at WellSpan Health York Hospital](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/IMG_5589-700x700.jpg)

![WellSpan York Hospital](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/08/IMG_5503.webp)

![WellSpan York Hospital](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/07/Wellspan_York_Hospital_Pedestrian_Bridge.webp)

![Healthcare design for medical facilities](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/IMG_5584-700x700.jpg)

![Demolition of older buildings on York Hospital campus to make way for expansion](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/IMG_5546-700x700.jpg)

![Groundbreaking at WellSpan Health York Hospital](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/IMG_5589-700x700.jpg)

![WellSpan York Hospital](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/08/IMG_5503.webp)

![WellSpan York Hospital](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/07/Wellspan_York_Hospital_Pedestrian_Bridge.webp)

![Healthcare design for medical facilities](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/IMG_5584-700x700.jpg)

## Extensive expansion at York Hospital

Since 1880, York Hospital has served the York community and has grown to become the regional’s leader in advanced specialty care. A multi-million-dollar expansion is underway, pushing the largest hospital in York County to become one of the top 10 largest hospitals in Pennsylvania. The project plans include an eight-story surgical and critical care tower, new women’s floor, ICU and operating rooms, all with state-of-the-art medical technology.

Project Details

Client

WellSpan Health

Size

Undisclosed

Completion Date

Estimated Spring 2025

Services

[Civil](https://warehausae.com/service/civil/)

Industries

[Healthcare](https://warehausae.com/industry/healthcare/)

![WellSpan York Hospital](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/08/IMG_5503-768x400.webp)

### We listen.

The project is expected to take five years to complete, with two older buildings on the campus being demolished to accommodate the expansion. Additional parking spaces will be added to the campus to provide convenient access for patients and visitors.

### We design.

Getting the project off to a successful start required detailed land planning and survey work. Had the project been in a green field, the design would have been straightforward. However, with an in-fill project located on an already fully developed campus with steep topography, the intricacies of implementing the client’s desired design were much more complex.

![Demolition of older buildings on York Hospital campus to make way for expansion](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/IMG_5546.jpg)

![Groundbreaking at WellSpan Health York Hospital](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/IMG_5589.jpg)

### We deliver.

Warehaus’ Civil team played a key role in coordination between subconsultants, two municipalities, the client and the design team. Subconsultants for the project include site survey, utility survey, geotechnical, environmental, and traffic related services. The design team includes Cannon Design, providing architectural and structural engineering services, and WSP, providing MEP engineering. Additionally, Warehaus prepared multiple concept plans and provided the following additional services: Zoning Variances, Land Development, NPDES Permitting, Construction Administration, and Post Construction Services.

# Preservation Pennsylvania Award Winner: Bischoff Inn

Located in the residential west end of the National Register listed Tamaqua Historic District, a former furniture factory from the 1870s now serves as the newest boutique hotel in town. With the guidance of Warehaus and Gina M. Douty, Historic Preservation Consulting, LLC from Mechanicsburg, owner Maria Stabio, who is also a Barnesville artist and photographer, spent the past few years converting the 6,000-sf building into a five-room venue known as the Bischoff Inn.

## Project Info

PROJECT LOCATION

Tamaqua, PA

Owner/Client

Torat, Ltd.

Project Type

Historic Preservation

Completion Date

### CONTEXT

Situated in the NPS registered Tamaqua Historic District, this 0.17-acre property consists of three contributing buildings, including the 2/3-story brick, main factory building, a 3-story framed lumber warehouse, and a 1 1/2-story framed wagon shed. Located in a residential setting, it is the earliest industrial complex of buildings surviving in the district.

Built circa 1872 and run by Conrad Bischoff, a cabinet maker from Bavaria, Germany, it operated as a family furniture business until 1930. They also had a funeral business that is still being run by descendants of the Bischoff family. The main factory building was a general store during the mid-20th century. Later, the buildings were used by the Becker Building Supplies company. The last use for the property was from the 1990s to about 2020 as the Lizard Creek Valley Antique Shop.

Over the last three years, the property was purchased and the main factory building was converted into a bed and breakfast now known as the Bischoff Inn. The project received community support resulting in a $900,000 City Revitalization & Improvement Zone reimbursement grant from the Borough of Tamaqua to rehabilitate the building. This funding source is administered by the PA Department of Revenue, PA DCED, and the Governor’s Office of the Budget. Federal Rehabilitation Investment Tax Credits were also received. There are future plans for the rear outbuildings as rental apartments or artists’ spaces. The former Conrad Bischoff Planing Mill & Furniture Factory stands as a testimony to the early industry of Tamaqua.

### CURRENT CONDITIONS

The historic value and condition of the structure was defined, as well as current conditions assessed. For the exterior, the ghost writing from historic advertisements of the past life of the building were preserved in its current state. The original wood windows were repaired in lieu of replacements, when possible. Only one or two rear windows needed replacement, and those were carefully matched in material and configuration of the originals. The original, first floor main entry doors were masterfully rebuilt to comply with safety and code regulations and painted to appear as they did in the late 1800s. Stairs leading to the doors were rebuilt to accommodate a safer entrance on arrival to the Inn, and the brick sidewalk fronting the main façade was re-laid to shed water away from the structure and provide a smoother walking surface. The wood shutters on the first floor, windows, and wood trim were repaired in kind and received fresh coats of paint.

### INTERIOR

The interior space was reconfigured to house bedrooms and individual bathrooms for visiting guests, as well as common space on the first floor, and an innkeeper’s suite on the second. Exposed brick, interior perimeter walls that contained window openings at the north and south were maintained as well as the original flooring to achieve the historic, industrial character of space. The west and east walls as well as the first-floor ceiling were covered with gypsum board where needed to allow for plumbing, electric, sprinkler systems, and HVAC pipes and chases to be concealed. Modern bathroom designs took on a whimsical, old fashion feel with fixtures and finishes complementing the historic character. The enclosed, historic stairs and door to the upper floor were kept in its present state, allowing for the historic character to be maintained along with historic graffiti of previous users and the original family members that still grace that area.

The original wood tongue and groove ceiling was maintained on the second floor, along with the walls, floors, and original tongue and groove dividing wall. Most HVAC equipment was housed in the attic and conduit run through closets and behind the new drywall. Mechanical systems that could not be located in the attic or walls presented a challenge as the original heights of the ceilings were to be maintained as much as possible. Lower profile ductwork and piping was then used and placed tightly between the floor and ceiling joists to achieve desired aesthetic. Another hurdle was designing new structural reinforcement to the framing system and proper restoration of the historic exposed stone foundation walls. The selected solution provided new strength to the building as new timber sections were coupled to the existing structural members.

### ARTISTIC TOUCHES

Lastly, the owner added her artistic touches to the finished spaces with original artwork, historic pieces of Tamaqua history, playful furnishings, and original wood headboards and tables in the bedrooms that were manufactured in the original furniture factory!

In January 2023, a ribbon cutting ceremony and public reception was held to officially open the Bischoff Inn for business. It is the hope of all those who worked on this project and for the town of Tamaqua that the investment in this historic property will be a success and bring people to the area to discover the history and unique experiences the town has to offer.

A later phase of work is planned for the property, including finishing the basement area as an entertainment space for guests, as well as a place to showcase artistic talents, and rehabilitating the rear historic two-story warehouse and two-story former wagon shed into rental apartments and artist community studio spaces.

Mindful collaboration occurred between a motivated and determined owner, historic preservation-minded designers and consultants, and builders sensitive and willing to preserve as much of the history and physical structure of the original furniture factory. This resulted in creative, thoughtful designs married to preservation and sympathetic construction methods that best created a purposeful building while deeply respecting its original historic sense of character, place, and time.

# Matt Robertson

Architectural Project Manager

Years in Industry: 15

Years with Warehaus: 9 total years

Matt is an Architectural Project Manager with nine years of experience at Warehaus, known for his team-oriented approach and steady project leadership. He creates space for ideas from across the team while keeping work aligned with a clear end goal, moving from big-picture thinking into refined detail. He adapts quickly as projects evolve and focuses on delivering successful outcomes for both the client and the team, approaching challenges with a solutions-first mindset that keeps projects moving.

Over the course of his career, Matt has contributed to a range of projects, with a particular focus on multi-family developments, where coordination and attention to detail are key. He is skilled in Revit, CAD, and other BIM software, along with 3D laser scanning and post-processing—tools he uses to support precise, efficient project delivery.

Outside of the office, Matt enjoys spending time with his family and being outdoors, where he’s an avid fly fisherman and hunter. He also designs and refinishes furniture in his free time.

# Travis Kepler, AIA

Architectural Process Director

Years in Industry: 25

Years with Warehaus: 14

Travis Kepler has been practicing architecture for over 25 years, bringing deep experience across education, museums, retail environments, assisted living, religious facilities, and medical practices. His work spans a range of construction types and delivery methods, with a consistent focus on projects that demand thoughtful coordination of building systems.

In his role as Architectural Process Director, Travis leads the design and documentation standards that shape every architectural project at Warehaus. He guides efforts to refine how drawings are developed, improve consistency across teams, and strengthen the integration of materials, codes, and construction methods. His work helps ensure that projects move forward with clarity, coordination, and a high level of quality.

Travis is particularly experienced in alternative learning environments, where performance requirements for systems such as heating, cooling, humidity, and lighting are critical. He also brings a strong background in adaptive reuse, with a focus on integrating modern systems into existing and historically sensitive structures.

His approach combines big-picture thinking with attention to detail, helping teams deliver work that is both well-designed and well-executed.

# David Koratich, EIT

Co-Owner / Chief Operating Officer

Years in Industry: 26

Years with Warehaus: 26

Along with Warehaus’ CEO Troy Bankert, David took ownership of the firm in 2021 and has been a part of the company since 1999.

Over his 26 years at Warehaus, David has partnered with Warehaus’ clients through the design and regulatory process for Civil/Site-related projects. He diversified the Civil Engineering Team’s portfolio to include commercial and industrial business parks, residential sub-divisions (single family, detached and high density), retail projects as well as professional offices.

David serves as Warehaus’ Chief Operating Officer, overseeing the day to-day administrative and operational functions of multiple business units including internal operations, information technology, building facilities, and business processes. David holds a degree in Civil Engineering from Penn State University.

## **We listen.** **We design.** **We deliver.**

# WAREHAUS

# retail & hospitality

## Hotel, Retail & Restaurant Design

Whether designing for discerning brands like Marriott or Hilton or repurposing history at decades-old hotels in York and Gettysburg, we draw on our well-rounded experience in [hospitality](http://warehausae.com/project/mchenry-row-office-hotel/), [workplace](http://paypal/) and [residential design](http://warehausae.com/project/nelson-kohl-apartments/) to create a distinct sense of place for each new project.

Our passion for sustainable design and incorporation of wellness spaces, both ever-present themes in hotel design, are also reflected in the many mixed-use residential projects in our portfolio. With a focus on upfront problem-solving, our team is dedicated to a quick and efficient process that achieves open-for-business deadlines.

- Full-Service Kitchen Design
- Expansions and Additions

- Furniture & Finish Selection
- Incorporation of Technology

## A Warehaus Partnership Brings

### A Creative Approach to Site Adaption

Your project site’s geography, climate and context is unique. We thoughtfully [adapt brand prototypes to your site](http://warehausae.com/project/mchenry-row-office-hotel/) and mitigate regulatory challenges so that your project kicks off successfully.

### Experience Working with Global Brands

like hospitality brands [Marriott](http://warehausae.com/project/courtyard-by-marriott-rowan-boulevard/), [Hilton](http://warehausae.com/project/hilton-garden-inn/) and Westin and retail giants like [Dunkin](http://warehausae.com/project/dunkin-east-retail/) and BJs mean we know their standards and protocols. We hit the ground running, saving you time and energy.

### A Relationship-Driven Team

We work hand-in-hand to guide our clients through an inclusive design process.

### Expertise in Building Science

Our expertise in [building materials](http://warehausae.com/work__trashed/building-sciences/) and systems and construction types creates high-performing buildings you can count on for decades.

> “The collaboration I have experienced with Warehaus, in my own backyard, has been a dream! Idea to fruition is a long creative process and the team there has made it not only fun, but possible in a big city way. A team is everything behind every project, and O.N.E. Is happy to have found our fit with Warehaus.”
>
> Toni Calderone
>
> O.N.E. Hospitality

# Kaltreider-Benfer Library: York County Library

## Red Lion, PA

![York County Library Kaltreider-Benfer Library Renovation York, PA](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/Kaltreider-Benfer-DRONE-13-700x700.webp)

![York County Library Kaltreider-Benfer Library Renovation York, PA](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/Kaltreider-Benfer-DRONE-3-700x700.webp)

![York County Library Kaltreider-Benfer Library Renovation York, PA](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/Kaltreider-Benfer-DRONE-3-700x700.webp)

![York County Library Kaltreider-Benfer Library Renovation York, PA](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/Kaltreider-Benfer-DRONE-9-700x700.webp)

![York County Library Kaltreider-Benfer Library Renovation York, PA](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/Kaltreider-Benfer-DRONE-7-700x700.webp)

![York County Library Kaltreider-Benfer Library Renovation York, PA](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/Kaltreider-Benfer-5-700x700.webp)

![York County Library Kaltreider-Benfer Library Renovation York, PA](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/Kaltreider-Benfer-6-700x700.webp)

![York County Library Kaltreider-Benfer Library Renovation York, PA](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/Kaltreider-Benfer-700x700.webp)

![York County Library Kaltreider-Benfer Library Renovation York, PA](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/Kaltreider-Benfer-2-700x700.webp)

## Imagine. Discover. Connect.

The Kaltreider-Benfer Library, part of York County Library System, unveiled its newly expanded and renovated space in 2023. This marks the culmination of a transformative journey. The Kaltreider-Benfer Library project is the crowning achievement of the “Imagine. Discover. Connect.” capital campaign involving dedicated volunteers, local support, matching grants, and financial assistance from the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania. This project is the last of the campaign’s renovations, which also included Kreutz Creek Library and Martin Library.

Project Details

Client

York County Library System

Size

13,358 sf

Completion Date

May 2023

Services

[Architecture](https://warehausae.com/service/architecture/), [Civil](https://warehausae.com/service/civil/), [Structural](https://warehausae.com/service/structural/)

Industries

[Arts and Culture](https://warehausae.com/industry/arts-and-culture/)

![York County Library Kaltreider-Benfer Library Renovation York, PA](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/Kaltreider-Benfer-DRONE-3.webp)

### We listen.

The library staff orchestrated the relocation of a staggering 45,000 books, shelves, and furniture to pave the way for construction. During this transition, the library continued to serve its patrons from a temporary site.

To prepare for construction, Warehaus’ civil engineering team performed work associated with a stormwater management plan that collected runoff from the existing and new roofs. They also provided recommendations for site improvements where curb and pavement were failing, and they worked to coordinate new utility connections.


### We design.

The $2 million renovation and construction initiative resulted in an impressive 3,676 sf expansion. This elevated the York County library’s footprint to a total of 13,358 sf. The library’s design prioritizes transparency, emphasizing outward views and public spaces. The children’s area promotes hands-on learning and sensory experiences. The the main browsing area offers diverse study spaces, reading nooks, and meeting spots. A dedicated teen area with collaborative workstations and seating is also included. Open spaces offer better visual quality from the main desk throughout the library. Mechanical systems and restrooms were also upgraded. These enhancements signify the library’s enduring commitment to community service.

![York County Library Kaltreider-Benfer Library Renovation York, PA](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/Kaltreider-Benfer-5.webp)

![York County Library Kaltreider-Benfer Library Renovation York, PA](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/Kaltreider-Benfer.webp)

### We deliver.

Robert Lambert, President of York County Library System remarked, “I think the community will embrace the three updated and reimagined libraries,” says Lambert. “It is with great pride that we unveil our third campaign project, Kaltreider-Benfer Library. The newly expanded and renovated library features dedicated children and teen spaces, an open service area, additional computers, seating, and meeting spaces.” Kaltreider-Benfer Library re-opened to the public in May 2023.

# Jeffrey Stough

Civil Project Manager

Years in Industry: 21

Years with Warehaus: 1

Jeff Stough brings over two decades of expertise in civil engineering, land development, and project management to Warehaus. As a Civil Project Manager, Jeff ensures that new projects are designed efficiently while adhering to local regulations. His role is pivotal in the development plan approval process, which involves close interaction with council members and government officials. His responsibilities also include creating detailed site development construction estimates, securing subcontractor quotes, managing permitting requirements.

Jeff is also Owner of Jaguar Commercial Real Estate, LLC, where he serves as Broker of Record for clients seeking sites for land development.

Jeff’s educational foundation includes coursework in Engineering Management with a Minor in Business Administration from York College of Pennsylvania and an Associate Degree in Design Drafting from York Technical Institute.

With a deep knowledge of civil, mechanical, and architectural drawings and a strong skill set in project planning and problem-solving, Jeff is an asset to the civil engineering team. His commitment to excellence and ability to navigate complex regulatory environments make him instrumental in driving successful project outcomes.

# Joseph Stein

Civil Project Director

Years in Industry: 20

Years with Warehaus: 7

Joseph Stein brings more than 19 years of experience to his role as Civil Project Director, where Joe is responsible for the overall success of civil project management – including efficiencies, workflow, and client management. From proposal to project completion, Joe is adept at working closely with clients to meet project schedules and provide expertise in creating E&S control plans, NPDES permitting, environmental permitting with DEP & USACE, sewage planning modules, stormwater, subdivision, master planning, and land development plan design. He’s worked closely with Municipalities, County and State government agencies, and has a long history with the City of York.

He has been a member of multiple volunteer boards in the City, including the Planning Commission, has participated in nearly a dozen playground builds with the City’s Bring on Play group, and is a member of the Rotary Club of York. Joe holds a degree in Environmental Science from Wilkes University and is also a York Catholic graduate.

# Derek Donnelly, PE

Structural Engineer

Years in Industry: 25

Years with Warehaus: 1

Derek Donnelly has been practicing structural engineering for more than 24 years, building expertise in structural building design and project management. His portfolio of work includes educational facilities, office buildings, apartment buildings, retail, hotels, churches, warehouses, manufacturing/industrial facilities, municipal buildings, single and multi-family residential buildings, wastewater treatment plants, renovation projects, and structural steel connection design. Derek also collaborated with the Warehaus team on many past projects.

A graduate of University of Pittsburgh-Johnstown with a degree in Civil Engineering Technology, Derek consistently demonstrates exceptional leadership and technical skills. Derek is licensed in Pennsylvania, Maryland, Virginia, West Virginia and Delaware.

## SEND A MESSAGE
