## **We listen.** **We design.** **We deliver.**

# WAREHAUS

# distribution & manufacturing

## We've designed more than 50M sf of distribution and manufacturing space

With hundreds of [warehouse](http://warehausae.com/project/ollies-bargain-outlet/), [distribution](http://warehausae.com/project/starbucks-coffee-roasting-plant/) and [production](http://warehausae.com/project/mobile-climate-control/) projects in our portfolio, we are committed to the goal of increasing productivity and minimizing operations costs at every industrial facility we design. Our solutions are driven by asking the right questions and working side-by-side with our clients’ operations and material handling experts, to learn their process from the inside out.

- Material Handling
- Efficiencies/Coordination
- Site Access and Traffic Flow
- Shipping and Receiving Patterns
- Concrete Slab Design
- Dock Optimization
- Security Access

- Lighting Efficiencies
- Office and Warehouse Integration
- Subconsultant Coordination and Management
- [Cold Storage/Dry Storage/Food Production Facilities](http://cold%20storage/)

## A Warehaus Partnership Brings

### A Multi-Disciplinary Team

With [architects](http://warehausae.com/services/architecture/) and [engineers](http://warehausae.com/services/structural/) under one roof, our comprehensive team has the skills needed to provide streamlined building evaluations and programming assessments. One team that can deliver in one site visit.

### Strong Relationships

Established relationships with local government enable us to understand and streamline approval processes. We listen carefully to our clients’ needs and navigate through regulatory requirements to ensure those needs are met efficiently and effectively.

### Collaboration as a Key Value

Our seamless working relationships with valued partners such as process engineers and material handling specialists ensures that your project team is aligned from day one.

### Speed to Market Efficiencies

Our tested and proven speed to market efficiencies translate to effective coordination on key issues like schedule and budget.

> “New and existing facilities are wonderfully unified in a program type that is rarely recognized for achieving design excellence. This design uses the simple but powerful qualities of natural daylight, visual access to vegetation and the outdoors, and direct and honest form to create a work environment that is generous and considerate of all employees.”
>
> AIA Central Pennsylvania Honor Award 2016
>
> Jury Comments: Phoenix Contact

## What our clients, partners, and community are saying about our work.

# York College of Pennsylvania Northside Commons Addition

## York, PA

![Northside Commons student housing expansion at York College - In Construction](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/Northside-Commons-Before-04082025-10-700x700.jpg)

![Northside Commons student housing expansion at York College - In Construction](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/Northside-Commons-Before-04082025-15-700x700.webp)

![Northside Commons student housing at York College](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/02U9864.jpg)

![Northside Commons student housing at York College](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/02U9893-700x700.webp)

![In Construction on Phase II of Northside Commons at York College of Pennsylvania](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/York_College_Northside_Commons_PhaseII20250718_0006-700x700.jpg)

![In Construction on Phase II of Northside Commons at York College of Pennsylvania](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/York_College_Northside_Commons_PhaseII20250718_0008-700x700.jpg)

![In Construction on Phase II of Northside Commons at York College of Pennsylvania](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/York_College_Northside_Commons_PhaseII20250718_0003-700x700.jpg)

![Northside Commons student housing expansion at York College - In Construction](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/Northside-Commons-Before-04082025-10-700x700.jpg)

![Northside Commons student housing expansion at York College - In Construction](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/Northside-Commons-Before-04082025-15-700x700.webp)

![Northside Commons student housing at York College](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/02U9864.jpg)

![Northside Commons student housing at York College](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/02U9893-700x700.webp)

![In Construction on Phase II of Northside Commons at York College of Pennsylvania](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/York_College_Northside_Commons_PhaseII20250718_0006-700x700.jpg)

![In Construction on Phase II of Northside Commons at York College of Pennsylvania](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/York_College_Northside_Commons_PhaseII20250718_0008-700x700.jpg)

![In Construction on Phase II of Northside Commons at York College of Pennsylvania](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/York_College_Northside_Commons_PhaseII20250718_0003-700x700.jpg)

## Building on Success at Northside Commons

In 2011, Warehaus successfully completed the design of Phase I of the Northside Commons project at York College, addressing the growing demand for student housing driven by increased enrollment. Strategically located to ensure seamless connectivity to the main campus, the five-story, 80,000 sf facility provides housing for 200 students and features common areas, lounge spaces, special instructional spaces, and a demonstration kitchen. The site plan also thoughtfully incorporated convenient access to parking, the regional greenway, and a network of footpaths linking the dormitory to the campus and surrounding community amenities.

Project Details

Client

York College of Pennsylvania

Size

60,965 sf

Completion Date

Projected Fall 2026

Services

[Architecture](https://warehausae.com/service/architecture/), [Civil](https://warehausae.com/service/civil/), [Structural](https://warehausae.com/service/structural/)

Industries

[Education](https://warehausae.com/industry/education/)

![Northside Commons student housing at York College](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/02U9893.webp)

### We listen.

As Phase II begins, Warehaus has once again been selected to design a significant expansion aimed at enhancing the existing dormitory. Our architectural, civil engineering, and structural engineering teams are collaborating to ensure the 60,965 sf dormitory wing addition accommodates the growing student population while enhancing campus living amenities. The site, adjacent to the Codorus Creek rail trail, was originally unused college land before the initial dormitory was constructed in the early 2010s. This project will include sidewalk improvements and address soil-related challenges such as erosion, low soil strength, and slow percolation. Erosion and sediment control measures, including rock construction entrances and compost filter socks, will be implemented to minimize earth disturbance and manage stormwater runoff.

### We design.

The architectural and structural teams conducted a thorough code review, confirming that minimal modifications were needed and that the exterior wall construction and insulation values from Phase I remain compliant with current building codes. This ensures consistency in energy efficiency and structural integrity across both phases. Additionally, Warehaus assessed the Phase I construction documents, materials, and layout to guide decisions for Phase II, leveraging insights from the initial phase to optimize the expansion project.

![Northside Commons student housing expansion at York College - In Construction](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/Northside-Commons-Before-04082025-15.webp)

![In Construction on Phase II of Northside Commons at York College of Pennsylvania](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/York_College_Northside_Commons_PhaseII20250718_0008-scaled.jpg)

### We deliver.

The design focuses on fostering a vibrant and inviting community environment for students. The new addition features a well-integrated layout that includes both double and single units, RA units, and communal restrooms. Additionally, the facility will offer diverse spaces such as bike storage, study rooms, laundry areas, kitchen spaces, and lounges to enhance resident convenience. Building upon the successes from Phase I, Warehaus aims to deliver a seamlessly integrated expansion that not only meets but exceeds the expectations of the campus community.

![Volvo North America Office Interiors](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Volvo-Construction-Equipment-10-700x700.jpg)

![Volvo North America Office Interiors](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Volvo-Construction-Equipment-261-HDR-700x700.jpg)

![Volvo North America Office Interiors](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Volvo-Construction-Equipment-43-700x700.jpg)

![Volvo North America Office Interiors](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Volvo-Construction-Equipment-185-HDR-700x700.jpg)

## A Flexible, Future-Driven Workplace for Volvo North America

Volvo North America transformed its traditional office space at its Shippensburg, Pennsylvania facility to better support evolving work dynamics. As remote work shifted many individual tasks outside the office, the company reimagined its workplace as a hub for collaboration and team-oriented activity. Spanning 84,723 sf, the facility emphasizes flexible, activity-based settings over individual offices and now serves as a model for future Volvo offices across Europe and the United States, aligned with the company’s industrial identity and brand colors.

Project Details

Client

Volvo North America

Size

84,723 sf

Completion Date

2025

Services

[Architecture](https://warehausae.com/service/architecture/), [Interiors](https://warehausae.com/service/interiors/)

Industries

[Office](https://warehausae.com/industry/office/)

![Volvo North America Office Interiors](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Volvo-Construction-Equipment-110-scaled.jpg)

### We listen.

Warehaus led the design and execution of this transformation, creating a workplace that incorporates agile furniture solutions tailored to a variety of work styles. The new office includes dedicated collaboration zones, privacy-focused call booths, and efficient meeting spaces, all reflecting Volvo’s industrial aesthetic. Flexible event spaces make the facility adaptable for large meetings, customer visits, and community engagement, while thoughtful wayfinding, industrial-themed décor, and curated art installations enrich the user experience.

### We design.

The renovation embraces Volvo’s “Living Office” concept, prioritizing collaboration, innovation, and employee well-being. The main entrance lobby is animated by a striking ceiling of wood-look planks interwoven with linear lighting, introducing depth, rhythm, and transparency. A layered reception desk of angled materials guides visitors toward secure access points, softened by planting and integrated seating to create a warm welcome. Beyond, a two-story geometric wood accent wall connects the first and second floors, illuminated naturally by skylights and dramatically at night with wall-grazing light. Together, these elements establish a dynamic arrival experience that balances functionality, security, and hospitality.

![Volvo North America Office Interiors](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Volvo-Construction-Equipment-282-HDR-scaled.jpg)

![Volvo North America Office Interiors](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Volvo-Construction-Equipment-152-scaled.jpg)

### We deliver.

Break areas, expanded fitness facilities, dedicated mother’s rooms, and provisions for future medical services further support a holistic workplace experience. Safety also guided the design, with careful attention to circulation patterns, clear lines of sight, and the strategic selection and placement of materials to support Volvo’s corporate Active Shooter Response Plan. By integrating these measures seamlessly into the environment, the design achieves a balance between open collaboration and secure workplace practices. The completed transformation positions Volvo’s Shippensburg facility as a forward-looking environment that unites innovation, adaptability, and a deep commitment to employee well-being and safety.

## Reflecting brand excellence in the design of a rustic, farmhouse style corporate office

Sterman Masser Inc. is known for its dedication to the agricultural sector, particularly in the realm of potato farming and processing. With a focus on innovation and sustainability, Sterman Masser has earned a reputation for excellence in producing high-quality potato products while also prioritizing environmental stewardship.

Project Details

Client

Sterman Masser, Inc.

Size

22,000 sf

Completion Date

In Process

Services

[Architecture](https://warehausae.com/service/architecture/), [Interiors](https://warehausae.com/service/interiors/)

Industries

[Office](https://warehausae.com/industry/office/)

![PICT0030](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/PICT0030-1-768x400.webp)

### We listen.

To reflect the excellence of their brand, the company sought to create a LEED-certified corporate headquarters in Sacramento, PA – a cutting-edge workspace to blend rustic industrial and farmhouse aesthetics with modern amenities and technologies. Warehaus was selected by Mowery Construction to partner on the project and provide programming and schematic design, interior design, and construction documents.

### We design.

Phase 1 of the 22,000 sf corporate office building will take the place of their current office structure, which is spread through a series of existing buildings and temporary mobile offices. The existing office spaces will be reincorporated back for use by the production facility and the mobile offices will be scheduled for demolition to gain area for a future project. The new design allows for future multi-floor wing expansions of an additional 15,000 sf of space. The headquarters’ aesthetic blends rustic industrial and modern farmhouse elements to blend with its rural environment. The design incorporates open vaulted office areas and large expanses of glazing to take advantage of natural light while emphasizing the optimal views of the existing mountain range, as well as the family farmed land. Additionally, the program incorporates a culinary cooking space for teaching and showcasing purposes.

![sterman-masser-hq_int-e](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/Sterman-Masser-HQ_int-e.webp)

![Sterman Masser Headquarters](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Sterman-Masser-HQ_ext-b.jpg)

### We deliver.

Warehaus will provide guidance on recommended wayfinding and monument signage throughout the headquarters, which includes both closed and open office spaces, seating areas, conference room, breakroom and training space, a first aid station, mailroom, and retail space.  Sterman Masser Family of Companies President, David Masser, said, “Working with the Warehaus team has been an exceptional experience, from the initial conception stage to the final result. The team thoroughly understood our requirements and translated them into a functional and visually stunning office space that perfectly caters to our needs.”

Photo Credit: ©Tom Holdsworth

## York Runs on Dunkin' at Susquehanna Trail

The Dunkin’ and Jimmy John’s project at Shoppes on the Trail, 3245 N Susquehanna Trail, is another project between Warehaus and longtime partner and Dunkin developer GN Realty. Located at a signalized intersection just a quarter mile west from the I-83 Emigsville Exit, the project transforms an existing underutilized site into a modern retail destination featuring a drive-thru and new construction designed for food, service, or retail-oriented tenants. Surrounded by a strong daytime employment population and a growing residential sector, the development offers high visibility, ample parking and multiple access points, making it a strategic location for both local and passing traffic. Warehaus provided the civil design for the project, ensuring the site was designed efficiently to meet municipal requirements while supporting a seamless integration with surrounding infrastructure.

Project Details

Client

GN Realty

Size

Completion Date

June 2025

Services

[Civil](https://warehausae.com/service/civil/), [Civil Engineering](https://warehausae.com/service/civil-engineering/)

Industries

[Retail and Hospitality](https://warehausae.com/industry/retail-and-hospitality/)

![getattachmentthumbnail-2024-11-26-090814](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/GetAttachmentThumbnail.webp)

### We listen.

The site was previously home to a local tavern that was razed years ago, leaving the property vacant and underutilized. This long-awaited redevelopment required zoning relief to allow for both a fast-food restaurant use and a drive-thru. Warehaus led the zoning process by preparing the necessary applications and representing the client through the zoning hearing, ultimately securing approvals for the proposed uses.

### We design.

A formal concept plan was then developed, and given the site’s size, additional retail space was incorporated into the design. To support the land development plan, Warehaus coordinated with survey, geotechnical, and traffic consultants, ensuring all aspects of site grading, utility connections, stormwater management, and road improvements were properly addressed. All necessary permits and approvals were obtained through Manchester Township and the York County Conservation District, including the site’s NPDES permit for stormwater management.

![Dunkin Susquehanna Trail Grand Opening](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/Susquehanna-Trail-Dunkin-and-Jimmy-Johns-1.webp)

![Dunkin Susquehanna Trail Grand Opening](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/Susquehanna-Trail-Dunkin-and-Jimmy-Johns-2.webp)

### We deliver.

Beyond zoning approvals, Warehaus ensured the project’s compliance with traffic and land development considerations. While a Traffic Analysis was not initially required, it was anticipated as part of the broader land development process to assess site access and traffic flow along North Susquehanna Trail; significant road improvements were ultimately required to facilitate safe traffic flow to and from the site. Warehaus facilitated discussions with municipal officials and attended key meetings to guide the project through regulatory approvals. With a trade area population exceeding 29,000 within a 3-mile radius and significant economic growth in the region, Shoppes on the Trail represents a well-positioned retail opportunity. Through strategic civil planning, the Dunkin & Jimmy Johns’ project is set to provide a functional, accessible, and well-integrated site that enhances the area’s retail and dining options.

## Investing in Safety, Service, and Community Access

With a growing population, increasing visitor traffic, and aging infrastructure, Cumberland Township identified a critical need to expand and modernize its municipal building and police department. Originally built in the late 1970s and last upgraded over 30 years ago, the facility no longer meets the operational demands of township staff or the safety needs of law enforcement and the public.

Project Details

Client

Cumberland Township

Size

Total 13,460 sf

Completion Date

Anticipated Spring 2026

Services

[Architecture](https://warehausae.com/service/architecture/), [Civil](https://warehausae.com/service/civil/), [Interiors](https://warehausae.com/service/interiors/)

Industries

[Municipal](https://warehausae.com/industry/municipal/), [Office](https://warehausae.com/industry/office/)

![Rendering of the Cumberland Township Municipal Building Renovation](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/Cumberland-Twp-Bldg-Back.webp)

### We listen.

To help realize this vision, Warehaus is serving as the architect for the comprehensive renovation and expansion of the municipal complex at 1370 Fairfield Road in Gettysburg, Pennsylvania. The project will address long-standing deficiencies—most notably, the police department’s inadequate workspace, where officers shared a cramped office and used a kitchenette located within a dark interior storage room.

### We design.

The design includes a new police wing featuring secure evidence storage, interview room, patrol room, and improved circulation for staff and visitors. The building’s garage side will be extended to form a new shared main entrance, and a gated carport that will provide covered parking for police vehicles. Interior renovations will enhance the township’s public meeting room, expand office space, and refresh finishes throughout, creating a more functional and professional environment.

![Overhead View of the Cumberland Township Municipal Building Site](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/Cumberland-Township-Before-03132025-12.jpg)

![Groundbreaking at the Cumberland Township Municipal Building, with Warehaus and Paragon Engineering](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/Cumberland_Township_Municipal_Building_Groundbreaking20250617_0029.webp)

### We deliver.

Upgrades also include a new roof, improvements to three auxiliary buildings on the site, a reconfigured parking layout, and a dedicated employee entrance to improve accessibility. Backed by more than $2.1 million in state grant funding—including RACP and Local Share Account awards—the project is being designed to meet grant compliance standards and deliver long-term value to the community. Construction is expected to be completed in early 2026, resulting in a modern municipal hub that better serves Cumberland Township’s residents, staff, and visitors.

## York County's history starts here

The York County History Center embarked on a transformative journey to celebrate the diverse historic experiences of York County, aiming to inspire future generations. The new museum project began with a multiyear consolidation, transitioning 10 scattered buildings to a single historical campus on the corner of North Pershing Avenue and Philadelphia Street and along the Codorus Creek in downtown York City.

Project Details

Client

York County History Center

Size

57,619 sf

Completion Date

June 2024

Services

[Architecture](https://warehausae.com/service/architecture/), [Historic](https://warehausae.com/service/historic/), [Interiors](https://warehausae.com/service/interiors/), [Structural](https://warehausae.com/service/structural/)

Industries

[Adaptive Reuse](https://warehausae.com/industry/adaptive-reuse/), [Arts and Culture](https://warehausae.com/industry/arts-and-culture/), [Historic](https://warehausae.com/industry/historic/)

![Two-story glass lobby connecting the steam plant to the adjacent building](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/York-County-History-Center_Rendering_Building-Approach.webp)

### We listen.

The former MetEd building was purchased in December 2015 with plans to convert the old steam plant into the historical hub of York County. The property, a significant historic building in York City dating back to 1885, is now a 50,000 sf modern museum connecting the steam plant to an adjacent building via a two-story lobby made mostly of glass. The team meticulously expanded and repurposed the historic industrial site, blending contemporary elements with its rich heritage.

The new history center includes a 15,000 sf main exhibit gallery, a 2,000 sf gallery for changing exhibits and a lobby that provides 3,000 to 4,000 sf for event space. The main exhibit gallery takes up most of the first floor of the steam plant, but portions of the gallery extend two stories high.

### We design.

The museum’s exhibits integrate large iconic artifacts, immersive environmental graphics, and interactive media. Themed around the notions of “BUILDING, GROWING, and MAKING,” the exhibits guide visitors through the county’s evolution, from its past accomplishments to its ongoing endeavors in renewal and innovation. The museum’s collection showcases an impressive array of macro-artifacts, ranging from a Conestoga wagon to a 72-ton ammonia compressor, each telling a unique story of York County’s residents and their contributions to history.

Behind the changing exhibit in the adjacent building is space for a multi-purpose café and warming kitchen to support events in the space. The second floor of the new center houses the library and archives, administrative offices and more exhibit space. A rooftop deck above the lobby and adjacent building provides additional event space and scenic views of downtown York City and the Codorus Creek. The third-floor space where giant bins once held coal for the power plant is converted into a community gallery with several thousand square feet of space for special collections.


![Warehaus Architect in York, PA - York County History Center Lobby](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/07/project-york-county-history-center20240625-tom-holdsworth0001-1-768x400.jpg)

![project_york-county-history-center20240625_tom_holdsworth0003](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/07/project-york-county-history-center20240625-tom-holdsworth0003-768x400.jpg)

### We deliver.

With demolition and site preparation commencing in June 2022 and the official groundbreaking in August of the same year, the York County History Center realized its vision with the project’s grand opening in June 2024. Photo Credit: ©Tom Holdsworth

# Gettysburg National Military Park Museum and Visitor Center

## Gettysburg, PA

![Refreshment saloon and bookstore at the Gettysburg National Military Museum & Visitor Center](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Gettysburg-National-Military-Museum-and-Visitor-Center-20080423_0167.jpg)

![Refreshment saloon and bookstore at the Gettysburg National Military Museum & Visitor Center](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Gettysburg-National-Military-Museum-and-Visitor-Center-20080423_0142.jpg)

![Facility able to store artifacts not on display, with catalog space and intake area for new items](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Gettysburg_National_Military_Museum_and_Visitor_Center_06-of-00.jpg)

![Refreshment saloon and bookstore at the Gettysburg National Military Museum & Visitor Center](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Gettysburg-National-Military-Museum-and-Visitor-Center-20080423_0167.jpg)

![Warehaus team providing programming, architecture, and engineering services for the Gettysburg National Military Museum & Visitor Center](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Gettysburg-National-Military-Museum-and-Visitor-Center-20080423_0123.jpg)

![Refreshment saloon and bookstore at the Gettysburg National Military Museum & Visitor Center](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Gettysburg-National-Military-Museum-and-Visitor-Center-20080423_0142.jpg)

![Design analysis for the Gettysburg National Military Museum & Visitor Center included wetland delineation and archaeological survey](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Gettysburg-National-Military-Museum-and-Visitor-Center-20080417_0231-700x700.webp)

![Building design upheld the desire to blend into the historic landscape of Gettysburg](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Gettysburg-National-Battlefield-Museum-and-Visitor-Center-07_10-0375.webp)

![Tours of the Gettysburg Battlefield available near the museum](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Gettysburg_National_Military_Museum_and_Visitor_Center_07-of-00.jpg)

![Museum Design in Gettysburg, Pennsylvania](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Gettysburg_National_Military_Museum_and_Visitor_Center_09-of-00-700x700.jpg)

![Multimedia experiential reenactment of the Battle of Gettysburg](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Gettysburg_National_Military_Museum_and_Visitor_Center_01-of-00.jpg)

## Creation of a multimedia experiential reenactment conveyed on the largest oil painting in North America

The Gettysburg National Military Museum & Visitor Center, a partnership between the National Park Service and the Gettysburg Foundation, is the largest and most successful public/private partnership of its kind. Working closely with an array of stakeholders at local, state and national levels, Warehaus helped to redefine the National Park Service’s model for museums and visitor centers with the master plan for the new museum, guaranteeing the preservation of the nation’s largest collection of civil war artifacts. Today, the popular center draws over 1.1 million visitors each year, engaging them with more than 20,000 square feet of interpretive space and two state-of-the-art theatres. Visitors are treated to a multimedia experiential reenactment of the Battle of Gettysburg, conveyed on the last remaining original Gettysburg Cyclorama, the largest oil painting in North America. The destination also showcases a world-class Civil War museum, refreshment saloon, bookstore and nearby tours of the battlefield.

Project Details

Client

The Gettysburg Foundation

Size

140,000 sf

Completion Date

2010

Services

[Architecture](https://warehausae.com/service/architecture/)

Industries

[Arts and Culture](https://warehausae.com/industry/arts-and-culture/)

![Design analysis for the Gettysburg National Military Museum & Visitor Center included wetland delineation and archaeological survey](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Gettysburg-National-Military-Museum-and-Visitor-Center-20080417_0231.webp)

### We listen.

Warehaus led the team, providing programming, architecture, master planning, civil engineering and environmental engineering services. The design analysis included wetland delineation, an archaeological survey, surveying services, joint permit design and coordination with State/ Federal agencies, land development permitting, construction oversight of improvements and monitoring of wetland mitigation areas. The building design upheld the desire to blend into the historic landscape and reinforce Gettysburg’s sense of place.

### We design.

With the ability to store artifacts that are not on display, the facility requires catalogue space and an intake area for new historical items. During construction, a vapor barrier was created, ensuring that external conditions do not cause a fluctuation in temperature and humidity inside. Construction needed to be phased in order to create a controlled working environment large enough to accommodate the Cyclorama.

![Multimedia experiential reenactment of the Battle of Gettysburg](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Gettysburg_National_Military_Museum_and_Visitor_Center_01-of-00.jpg)

![Tours of the Gettysburg Battlefield available near the museum](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Gettysburg_National_Military_Museum_and_Visitor_Center_07-of-00.jpg)

### We deliver.

The Gettysburg National Military Park Museum and Visitor Center was designed to meet LEED performance certification standards and was awarded Gold Level Certification in 2010, especially significant given the typical energy consumption of museum facilities due to exacting standards required for preservation.

# York Academy Regional Charter School – Lower School

## York, PA

![Exterior of historic York Academy Regional Charter School building](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/York-Academy-Regional-Charter-Upper-School_Professional-093011_00101.webp)

![Visually rich learning environment at York Academy Regional Charter School](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/York-Academy-Regional-Charter-Upper-School_Professional-081111_00060.webp)

![Preserved timber and brick construction inside York Academy Regional Charter School](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/York-Academy-Regional-Charter-Upper-School_Professional-101711_00008.jpg)

![Exterior of historic York Academy Regional Charter School building](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/York-Academy-Regional-Charter-Upper-School_Professional-093011_00104.webp)

![Visually rich learning environment at York Academy Regional Charter School](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/York-Academy-Regional-Charter-Upper-School_Professional-081111_00058.webp)

![K-8 students at York Academy Regional Charter School](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/York-Academy-Regional-Charter-Upper-School_Professional-052419_00084.jpg)

![Exterior of historic York Academy Regional Charter School building](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/York-Academy-Regional-Charter-Upper-School_Professional-093011_00101.webp)

![Visually rich learning environment at York Academy Regional Charter School](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/York-Academy-Regional-Charter-Upper-School_Professional-081111_00060.webp)

![Preserved timber and brick construction inside York Academy Regional Charter School](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/York-Academy-Regional-Charter-Upper-School_Professional-101711_00008.jpg)

![Exterior of historic York Academy Regional Charter School building](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/York-Academy-Regional-Charter-Upper-School_Professional-093011_00104.webp)

![Visually rich learning environment at York Academy Regional Charter School](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/York-Academy-Regional-Charter-Upper-School_Professional-081111_00058.webp)

![K-8 students at York Academy Regional Charter School](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/York-Academy-Regional-Charter-Upper-School_Professional-052419_00084.jpg)

## A thriving academic space emerges from an adaptive reuse transformation

Located in the heart of downtown York, the York Academy Regional Charter School was designed to bring together York City and surrounding county students in an enriched academic environment.

Project Details

Client

York Academy Regional Charter School

Size

86,000 sf

Completion Date

2015

Services

[Architecture](https://warehausae.com/service/architecture/), [Civil](https://warehausae.com/service/civil/), [Historic](https://warehausae.com/service/historic/), [Interiors](https://warehausae.com/service/interiors/)

Industries

[Adaptive Reuse](https://warehausae.com/industry/adaptive-reuse/), [Education](https://warehausae.com/industry/education/), [Historic](https://warehausae.com/industry/historic/)

![Groundbreaking at the York Academy Regional Charter School](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/York-Academy-Regional-Charter-Upper-School_Professional-041411_00037.webp)

### We listen.

Warehaus’ civil engineering team used their extensive experience to secure proper zoning and land entitlements for the site. Retaining the historical integrity of the building’s exterior was also paramount. Working hand in hand with the City of York Historical Review Board (HARB), Warehaus’ historic preservation experts planned for the entire building to be cleaned, repointed and re-stabilized.

### We design.

Five phases of construction were scheduled to allow the academy to add one grade per year, until it reaches full implementation as a K-12 school. Phase I was expedited to open K-2 grades in the 2011-2012 academic year. The design accentuates the historic 1840s-style roof and vintage windows to enhance the building’s charm. The interior was designed to showcase as much of the building’s existing timber and brick construction as possible.

![Exterior of historic York Academy Regional Charter School building](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/York-Academy-Regional-Charter-Upper-School_Professional-093011_00101.webp)

![Visually rich learning environment at York Academy Regional Charter School](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/York-Academy-Regional-Charter-Upper-School_Professional-081111_00058.webp)

### We deliver.

In a later phase, a three-story addition, including new administrative offices and an elevator, was completed in 7 months. The resulting facility now houses K-8 grades in a visually rich learning environment that enhances York Academy’s mission for academic excellence.

## Full-scope civil engineering services along the busy I-83 corridor

Since 2008, development along the I-83 corridor in York County, Pennsylvania has been constant, including Phases I and II of Orchard Business Park in East Manchester Township.

Project Details

Client

Excel, Inc. d/b/a DHL Supply Chain (USA)

Size

Site of 1,000,000 sf warehouse

Completion Date

2019

Services

[Civil](https://warehausae.com/service/civil/)

Industries

[Distribution and Manufacturing](https://warehausae.com/industry/distribution-and-manufacturing/)

![Subdivision and Land Development services provided for warehouse project along I-83](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Screenshot-2023-05-01-at-8.56.20-AM.jpg)

### We listen.

Adding to this collective along the I-83 corridor is a 1,000,000 sf warehouse located at 84 Zions View Road.  Situated on approximately 100 acres, developing the project required subdivision and land development permitting through East Manchester Township, a General NPDES permit with erosion and sediment control plan approval from the York County Conservation District and a PennDOT Highway Occupancy Permit for extensive roadway improvements both at the site and offsite. Warehaus also provided planning and design of a public sewer extension through Manchester Street.

### We design.

Prior to development, Warehaus provided site pre-design services to determine the lot configuration and site layout for the proposed development. Our team reviewed and summarized the Municipal Ordinances and prepared conceptual sketches, grading plan sketches and an analysis of potential environmental impacts each layout could encounter.

![Subdivision and Land Development services provided for warehouse project along I-83](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Screenshot-2023-05-01-at-8.55.17-AM.jpg)

![Subdivision and Land Development services provided for warehouse project along I-83](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Screenshot-2023-05-01-at-4.06.42-PM.jpg)

### We deliver.

The project required Warehaus to provide extensive oversight and construction management services throughout the build which had an extremely tight time schedule.  Construction was completed in less than one year.

# Milton and Catherine Hershey Conservatory at Hershey Gardens

## Hershey, PA

![Milton and Catherine Hershey Grand Conservatory design](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Warehaus_HersheyConsv_MMVisuals-0044.jpg)

![Full height Palladian-style windows with garden view](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Warehaus_HersheyConsv_MMVisuals-0024.jpg)

![Historic rose garden view from Hershey Conservatory](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Warehaus_HersheyConsv_MMVisuals-0054.jpg)

## A spectacular entrance to the Hershey Gardens, including the butterfly atrium feature attraction

The Hershey Gardens were founded by Milton Hershey in 1937, beginning as a 3-1/2 acre rose garden. Growing to 23 acres, the site is now home to the 14,000 sf Milton & Hershey Conservatory, completed in 2015. Perched atop the Hershey Gardens and overlooking the historic rose garden, its design harkens back to the conservatories Milton & Catherine Hershey built in the early 1900s. True to the Hershey’s commitment to enrich their community and provide delight to young learners, the Conservatory offers stunning horticulture exhibits and a dynamic butterfly atrium for visitors of all ages.

Project Details

Client

The M.S. Hershey Foundation

Size

14,000 sf

Completion Date

2016

Services

[Architecture](https://warehausae.com/service/architecture/), [Interiors](https://warehausae.com/service/interiors/)

Industries

[Arts and Culture](https://warehausae.com/industry/arts-and-culture/)

![Aerial View of Hershey Conservatory and Gardens](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Warehaus_HersheyConsv_MMVisuals-0011.jpg)

### We listen.

The design of the new structure mirrors the historic detail of Milton & Catherine Hershey’s original conservatory at their family home, High Point. It also incorporates modern materials, systems and functionality to support the intricacies of the project program. Guests are greeted in the 2,800 sf Visitor Center Welcome Pavilion, featuring eight floor-mounted planters with 20’ tropical trees and flowing foliage reaching toward a large skylight, adorned with hanging stained-glass butterflies. Full height Palladian-style windows provide sweeping views of the gardens.

### We design.

The visitor center leads into the main attraction, a 2,000 sf living tropical butterfly atrium exhibit. This specialized indoor environment allows visitors to view tropical butterflies flourishing year-round. Both the envelope and interior conditions were carefully designed to maintain the tight environmental tolerances that encourage high levels of butterfly movement. To encourage flight activity, the design incorporates smooth transitions of the surface materials and eliminates sharp corners. A separate chrysalis exhibit is environmentally controlled to showcase the hatching process, and the adjacent workroom is a “clean room” with a bright white finish for easy identification of very small parasites.

![Living tropical butterfly atrium exhibit](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Grand-Conservatory-at-Hershey_Professional-20160914_0023.jpg)

![Retail gift shop in the Conservatory](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Grand-Conservatory-at-Hershey_Professional-20160914_0026.jpg)

### We deliver.

The Conservatory also incorporates a 450 sf retail gift shop and 2,200 sf of flexible event space that serves rotating seasonal exhibits, educational support learning stations and wedding events. Lower level support spaces include 1,300 sf of office space, public restrooms, FDA-controlled shipping and receiving areas and mechanical equipment space and storage for exhibits.

# Mobile Climate Control

## York, PA

![Mobile Climate Control bus HVAC system production facility in York, Pennsylvania](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Mobile-Climate-Control-34-700x700.jpg)

![Mobile Climate Control distribution and manufacturing production facility in York, Pennsylvania](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Mobile-Climate-Control-22-700x700.jpg)

![Mobile Climate Control bus HVAC system production facility in York, Pennsylvania](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Mobile-Climate-Control-9-700x700.jpg)

![Mobile Climate Control bus HVAC system production facility in York, Pennsylvania](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Mobile-Climate-Control-50.jpg)

## Warehaus' multi-disciplinary team collaborates on design

Mobile Climate Control develops customized HVAC systems for city, school and tour buses. Their newest bus heating and A/C production facility opened in York, Pennsylvania in 2022 – with Warehaus performing the civil, architectural and structural design of the new facility.

Project Details

Client

Kinsley Properties

Size

220,000 sf

Completion Date

2022

Services

[Architecture](https://warehausae.com/service/architecture/), [Civil](https://warehausae.com/service/civil/), [Structural](https://warehausae.com/service/structural/)

Industries

[Distribution and Manufacturing](https://warehausae.com/industry/distribution-and-manufacturing/)

![Mobile Climate Control bus HVAC system production facility in York, Pennsylvania](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Mobile-Climate-Control-34.jpg)

### We listen.

The new 220,000 sf office/warehouse/fabrication facility houses 20,000 sf of office space, including conference spaces, canteens, and a training room. The warehouse and manufacturing area includes lab space, a machine shop, production lines, and multiple loading docks. The building was also designed for a future expansion of 120,000 sf.

### We design.

Structurally, the design incorporates tilt-up concrete bearing/lateral load resisting panels and a steel joist/beam/joist girder roof with interior steel columns. All structures are supported on conventional shallow spread footings.

![Mobile Climate Control bus HVAC system production facility in York, Pennsylvania](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Mobile-Climate-Control-2.jpg)

![Mobile Climate Control bus HVAC system production facility in York, Pennsylvania](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/MCC-Screen-Shot-2022-10-14-at-12.41.23-PM.jpg)

### We deliver.

Warehaus’ Civil Team secured Land Development & General NPDES Permits ahead of schedule, while also provided Bidding, Construction Administration and Post Construction related services. Site amenities included stormwater design for the entire build out, 177 employee parking spaces, 30 loading docks, 4 drive-in tractor trailer doors and site landscaping.

![Barn wood focal wall in lobby of Procter & Gamble Tabler Station Plant](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Procter-Gamble-Tabler-Station-Plant-0621201919-of-36.jpg)

![Procter & Gamble Tabler Station Plant 796,320 sf warehouse and distribution facility](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Procter-Gamble-Tabler-Station-Plant-0621201931-of-36.jpg)

## Sustainable design principles on display inside and out

Procter & Gamble’s manufacturing plant in West Virginia is the largest P&G production facility in the world. Warehaus provided Architectural and Interior Design services for the 796,320 sf warehouse and distribution facility, along with a 30,000 sf Resource Center and Administration Building and Gatehouse and Trucker’s Lounge.

Project Details

Client

Procter & Gamble

Size

796,320 sf Warehouse; 30,000 sf Resource Center and Admin Building

Completion Date

2018

Services

[Architecture](https://warehausae.com/service/architecture/), [Interiors](https://warehausae.com/service/interiors/)

Industries

[Distribution and Manufacturing](https://warehausae.com/industry/distribution-and-manufacturing/), [Office](https://warehausae.com/industry/office/)

![P&G manufacturing plant in West Virginia](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Procter-Gamble-Live-Wall-0621201956-of-57.jpg)

### We listen.

Collaborating with all project partners early and often is the key to keeping large scale projects on track. Warehaus partnered closely with Procter
& Gamble to ensure all aspects of the multi-faceted project met client expectations.

### We design.

Amenities across all projects include a cafeteria, full-service kitchen, campus server room, showers, office and conference space and a nurse’s suite. Warehaus managed vendor coordination to create the full-service kitchen. The design also incorporated manufacturing equipment from various P&G partners.

![Barn wood focal wall in lobby of Procter & Gamble Tabler Station Plant](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Procter-Gamble-Tabler-Station-Plant-0621201914-of-36.jpg)

![LEED Silver certification at Procter & Gamble Tabler Station](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Procter-Gamble-Live-Wall-0621201953-of-57.jpg)

### We deliver.

The plant is LEED Silver certified and incorporates sustainable design throughout. A main focal wall in the lobby utilizes barn wood salvaged from the original site. And a living wall makes a statement on the exterior of the building.

![Spacious vestibule leading to Kreutz Creek Library](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Kreutz-Creek-Library-06-700x700.jpg)

![State-of-the-art Kreutz Creek Library in York County](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Kreutz-Creek-Library-26.jpg)

![Comfortable lounge seating at Kreutz Creek Library](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Kreutz-Creek-Library-09-700x700.jpg)

![Comfortable lounge seating at Kreutz Creek Library](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Kreutz-Creek-Library-15.jpg)

![Comfortable lounge seating at Kreutz Creek Library](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Kreutz-Creek-Library-21.jpg)

![Play space and window seats in the children's area at Kreutz Creek Library](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Kreutz-Creek-Library-01.jpg)

![Children's Area at New Kreutz Creek Library](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Kreutz-Creek-Library-05.jpg)

![State-of-the-art Kreutz Creek Library in York County](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/2022_03_21_0236-Edit.jpg)

![State-of-the-art Kreutz Creek Library in York County](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/2022_03_21_0245-Edit.jpg)

![Outdoor patio and garden at Kreutz Creek Library](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/2022_03_21_0250-Edit.jpg)

![Spacious vestibule leading to Kreutz Creek Library](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Kreutz-Creek-Library-06-700x700.jpg)

## From a 40-year-old FEMA trailer to a state-of-the-art community library

As the foundation of the community, libraries help people meet their highest potential. York County Libraries mission to provide access to education, enrichment and technology is propelled by their announcement of a visionary campaign that will prepare their libraries for 21st century learning in state-of-the-art facilities where everyone feels safe, it is free and is available to all segments of the community. Martin Library, Kreutz Creek Library and Kaltreider-Benfer Library are all poised for enhancements designed by Warehaus’ architectural team.

Project Details

Client

York County Libraries

Size

5,550 sf

Completion Date

2022

Services

[Architecture](https://warehausae.com/service/architecture/)

Industries

[Arts and Culture](https://warehausae.com/industry/arts-and-culture/)

![Former FEMA trailer housing Kreutz Creek Library for 40 years](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Kreutz-Creek-Library-23.jpg)

### We listen.

Warehaus has been a partner of the YCL system for many years, and provided an analysis of the physical conditions and a repair/maintenance plan for each of the six buildings in the library system. This report drives the York Country Library Systems’ facility management maintenance strategy, including the Kreutz Creek Library building. Since the 1970’s, the library was housed in a former FEMA trailer that the library had outgrown. After 40 years, the dream of a bigger library has come to fruition with the revealing of the new Kreutz Creek Library.

### We design.

The new library is seven times larger than the previous FEMA trailer, making room for numerous enhancements, including more book shelving, seating, and computers and ample windows to let in natural light. The entrance guides visitors into a spacious vestibule leading into a huge circulation and welcome desk. Visitors can enjoy comfortable lounge seating and new quiet rooms for studying and creating – with monitors and white boards. A new dedicated teen space provides comfortable seating, wifi access and device chargers, space for events, and a mini maker-space cart with a variety of supplies. The children’s area features play space, window seats, and access to outdoor space with a fenced-in patio and garden.

![State-of-the-art Kreutz Creek Library in York County](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/2022_03_21_0236-Edit.jpg)

![Spacious vestibule leading to Kreutz Creek Library](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Kreutz-Creek-Library-06.jpg)

### We deliver.

The staff can enjoy enhancements including new offices, a work-break room and climate-controlled space for donated item storage and preparation for events. Kreutz Creek Library Branch Manager Susan Nenstiel commented, “Library staff, friends of the library, volunteers, donors and the local community – everyone is excited to take part in and witness this exciting time in Kreutz Creek Library’s history.” The library opened in early 2022.

# Capitol Theatre

## York, PA

![Renovations at the Capitol Theatre at the Appell Center for Performing Arts](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/07/Capitol_Theatre-11_2018-0001.jpg)

![Renovations at the Capitol Theatre at the Appell Center for Performing Arts](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/07/Strand-Capitol-Historic_Professional-052119_00766-700x700.jpg)

![Renovations at the Capitol Theatre at the Appell Center for Performing Arts](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/07/Capitol_Theatre-11_2018-0000-700x700.jpg)

![Renovations at the Capitol Theatre at the Appell Center for Performing Arts](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/07/Strand-Capitol-Historic_Professional-052119_00765-700x700.jpg)

![Renovations at the Capitol Theatre at the Appell Center for Performing Arts](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/07/Capitol_Theatre-11_2018-0000-700x700.jpg)

## Creation of a modern entertainment experience at a treasured York landmark

Originally built in 1906 as a one level dance hall, the Capitol Theatre is now home to a 450-seat venue hosting more than 125 entertainment and performing arts events each year. Efforts to secure the theater’s ability to serve York’s modern entertainment needs make for a more vibrant and economically flourishing downtown.

Project Details

Client

Strand Capitol Performing Arts Center

Size

450 seats

Completion Date

2018

Services

[Architecture](https://warehausae.com/service/architecture/)

Industries

[Arts and Culture](https://warehausae.com/industry/arts-and-culture/), [Historic](https://warehausae.com/industry/historic/)

![Renovations at the Capitol Theatre at the Appell Center for Performing Arts](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/07/Strand-Capitol-Historic_Professional-052119_00765.jpg)

### We listen.

Setting the foundation for the renovation work, Warehaus surveyed the interior of the theatre to confirm existing conditions and gain a more detailed view of areas in need of extensive intervention. Warehaus then designed thoughtful upgrades throughout.

### We design.

By working with the seating manufacturer to confirm balcony re-rake and revised sight lines, every balcony seat now allows for a full view of the stage. On both the upper and lower levels, seating is now more comfortable, and a sound wall on the lower level eliminates light and noise disruptions. The lobby and concessions areas are expanded and include a ground floor ADA accessible restroom. Theater walls and ceiling were re-painted and plaster was replaced to provide a fresh new look.

![Renovations at the Capitol Theatre at the Appell Center for Performing Arts](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/07/Strand-Capitol-Historic_Professional-052119_00766.jpg)

![Renovations at the Capitol Theatre at the Appell Center for Performing Arts](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/07/Capitol_Theatre-11_2018-0000.jpg)

### We deliver.

Warehaus’ MEP engineers reviewed existing conditions and designed plumbing, HVAC, electrical and lighting for lower level restrooms, lobby and concessions areas. Lighting upgrades within the theatre included design of egress lighting and emergency backup ballasts. They also implemented a fully-compliant NFPA 13 sprinkler system and a NFPA 72 fire alarm system.

![George Spangler Farm Historic Restoration in Gettysburg, PA](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/George_Spangler_Farm_20191101_0050-1.webp)

![George Spangler Farm Summer Kitchen and Barn Before Restoration](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/George-Spangler-Farm-Before-03_2012-0004.jpg)

![Comprehensive Historic Structures Report by Warehaus and Keystone Preservation](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Project_George-Spangler20160817_0009.jpg)

![A Deteriorated George Spangler Farm Barn Before Restoration](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/George-Spangler-Barn2012-2013_09-of-317-1.jpg)

## Winner of a 2020 Preservation Pennsylvania Historic Preservation Award

The George Spangler Farmstead, listed on the National Register of Historic Places, is located within the boundary of the Gettysburg National Military Park and served as a Union artillery staging area, corps hospital, and burial ground during the Battle of Gettysburg. Today, the 80-acre farmstead is maintained by the Gettysburg Foundation. Four buildings with great historical integrity remain, including the farmhouse, barn, summer kitchen and a smokehouse.

Project Details

Client

The Gettysburg Foundation

Size

90 acres; 4 buildings

Completion Date

2019

Services

[Architecture](https://warehausae.com/service/architecture/), [Historic](https://warehausae.com/service/historic/), [Interiors](https://warehausae.com/service/interiors/)

Industries

[Adaptive Reuse](https://warehausae.com/industry/adaptive-reuse/), [Arts and Culture](https://warehausae.com/industry/arts-and-culture/), [Historic](https://warehausae.com/industry/historic/), [Office](https://warehausae.com/industry/office/)

![George Spangler Farmstead Historic Preservation](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/Warehaus_GeorgeSpanglerFarm-0032.webp)

### We listen.

The Gettysburg Foundation aimed to explore opportunities to use the grounds as a living history museum and to place a training center on the property. To prepare for those additions, Warehaus worked with Keystone Preservation, Inc., to develop a comprehensive Historic Structures Report for the site, which included extensive research documenting the history of the farm and the role it played during the Battle of Gettysburg.

### We design.

Physical investigations and selective demolition helped to determine the farm’s appearance during 1863. The significance, integrity, character- defining features, and existing conditions of each building on site were documented. Several options for re-use of the farmstead were explored, and a recommendation was made based on an evaluation of the farmstead’s history.

![Structural support at George Spangler Farm in Gettysburg, PA](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/George_Spangler_Farm_20191101_0076.jpg)

![Barn, Summer Kitchen, and Smokehouse meticulously restored for interpretive tours](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/George_Spangler_Farm_20191101_0045.jpg)

### We deliver.

Warehaus partnered with the Gettysburg Foundation to restore the farm to its 1863 condition. The Barn, Summer Kitchen, and Smokehouse were meticulously restored for use in interpretive tours of the farmstead.

# Catalyst Muddy Creek Warehouse

## Denver, PA

## A Warehouse Project Driven by Collaboration

Working together with Catalyst Commercial Development and Warfel Construction, Warehaus provided the architectural and structural design for a 170,000 sf warehouse positioned on 13 acres along major transportation routes in Lancaster County, Pennsylvania.

Project Details

Client

Warfel Construction

Size

170,000 sf

Completion Date

May 2023

Services

[Architecture](https://warehausae.com/service/architecture/), [Structural](https://warehausae.com/service/structural/)

Industries

[Distribution and Manufacturing](https://warehausae.com/industry/distribution-and-manufacturing/)

![Architectural and structural design at Catalyst Commercial Development Muddy Creek warehouse](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/03/Catalyst-Muddy-Creek-10-768x400.webp)

### We listen.

The project involved designing a spacious warehouse with areas for a fire pump and electrical access. Additionally the design incorporates finished office space for a complete solution.

### We design.

Structurally, Warehaus designed the project using 122 precast concrete panels for the exterior walls and conventional steel framing for the roof and columns, enabling the construction partner to work quickly without compromising quality.

![Architectural and structural design at Catalyst Commercial Development Muddy Creek warehouse](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/03/Catalyst-Muddy-Creek-14-768x400.webp)

![Architectural and structural design at Catalyst Commercial Development Muddy Creek warehouse](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/03/Catalyst-Muddy-Creek-6-768x400.webp)

### We deliver.

The warehouse was completed in May 2023, providing ideal transportation access to the Pennsylvania Turnpike and US Route 222.

# Little Round Top

## Gettysburg, PA

![Site Plan for Little Round Top Battlefield in Gettysburg National Military Park](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Little_Round_Top_1.jpg)

![Site Plan for Little Round Top Battlefield in Gettysburg National Military Park](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Little-Round-Top_Rendering-20160914_0000.jpg)

![Little Round Top Battlefield in Gettysburg National Military Park](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Little_Round_Top_2-700x700.jpg)

![Site Plan for Little Round Top Battlefield in Gettysburg National Military Park](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Little_Round_Top_1.jpg)

![Site Plan for Little Round Top Battlefield in Gettysburg National Military Park](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Little-Round-Top_Rendering-20160914_0000.jpg)

![Little Round Top Battlefield in Gettysburg National Military Park](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Little_Round_Top_2-700x700.jpg)

![Site Plan for Little Round Top Battlefield in Gettysburg National Military Park](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Little_Round_Top_1.jpg)

![Site Plan for Little Round Top Battlefield in Gettysburg National Military Park](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Little-Round-Top_Rendering-20160914_0000.jpg)

![Little Round Top Battlefield in Gettysburg National Military Park](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Little_Round_Top_2-700x700.jpg)

## Capturing the essence of the battlefield experience while accentuating key moments

A key historical landmark in the Battle of Gettysburg in 1863, Little Round Top is one of Gettysburg National Military Park’s most heavily visited sites. Current visitation levels far exceed the capacity of Little Round Top’s trails and roadways. Visitors often stray from authorized trails, causing damage to natural and cultural resources. Overcrowding can also compromise the visitor experience. Parking configurations and congestion create conditions that are dangerous for pedestrians crossing the roadways.

Project Details

Client

The Gettysburg Foundation

Size

70 acres

Completion Date

Warehaus’ contributions were completed in 2016

Services

[Architecture](https://warehausae.com/service/architecture/), [Civil](https://warehausae.com/service/civil/)

Industries

[Historic](https://warehausae.com/industry/historic/)

![Site Plan for Little Round Top Battlefield in Gettysburg National Military Park](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Little_Round_Top_1.jpg)

### We listen.

To address these concerns, The Gettysburg Foundation commissioned a cultural landscape report that sought solutions for overuse, overcrowding and landscape degradation. Warehaus partnered with The Gettysburg Foundation to create an architectural and site plan and budget to help secure funding for the improvements.

### We design.

The schematic design focused on improving approximately 1.5 miles of trails system. Surface treatment selections and widths for each trail were based on a hierarchy of the trail system. In addition, the schematic design focused on constructing 10 observation decks and additional gathering areas to displace large groups off the trail system during tours. Due to the historic significance of the terrain, each element proposed was unique in nature to ensure the minimal amount of ground would be disturbed during construction.

![Site Plan for Little Round Top Battlefield in Gettysburg National Military Park](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Little-Round-Top_Rendering-20160914_0000.jpg)

![Little Round Top Battlefield in Gettysburg National Military Park](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Little_Round_Top_2.jpg)

### We deliver.

Warehaus’ civil engineering team also created plans that outlined increased parking at the summit to improve circulation for both vehicular and bus traffic. Throughout the site, crosswalks were recommended to give visitors a safe path of travel. The civil team coordinated the schematic design to extend public utilities over a mile, to a proposed comfort station, which converted and expanded an existing schoolhouse into a rest space with vehicular and bus parking. Additionally, the team researched and discussed many different surface materials with the client, to minimize stormwater runoff on the new trail system, where erosion issues caused an ongoing threat.

# Northern Gateway

## York, PA

![Northern Gateway Renderings York, PA](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/2019-3D-View-INTERIOR-PERSPECTIVE-VIEW-1-700x700.jpg)

![Northern Gateway Renderings York, PA](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/2019-3D-View-INTERIOR-PERSPECTIVE-VIEW-2.jpg)

![Northern Gateway Renderings York, PA](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/2019-3D-View-INTERIOR-PERSPECTIVE-VIEW-1-700x700.jpg)

![Northern Gateway Renderings York, PA](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/2019-3D-View-INTERIOR-PERSPECTIVE-VIEW-2.jpg)

## A gateway to York City

At the corner of North George Street and West North Street sits an unoccupied complex of historic buildings, one of the last properties fit for revitalization along the Northern Gateway to York City. Cattycorner to the York Revolution baseball stadium, this Northern Gateway property is within HARB review jurisdiction.

Project Details

Client

Royal Square Development, Mexitaly

Size

11,842 sf

Completion Date

In Process

Services

[Architecture](https://warehausae.com/service/architecture/), [Structural](https://warehausae.com/service/structural/)

Industries

[Adaptive Reuse](https://warehausae.com/industry/adaptive-reuse/), [Historic](https://warehausae.com/industry/historic/)

![Northern Gateway Renderings York, PA](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/2019-3D-View-EXTERIOR-PERSPECTIVE-VIEW-2.jpg)

### We listen.

Working with the developer and the restaurant owner, Warehaus assisted communication between parties. They facilitated a communication plan to ensure the developers’ needs for the structure and utility infrastructure were solidified and met the property managers’ requirements. The plans also supported the restaurant operator in regard to flow and equipment. In addition, Warehaus planned broadly to ensure the facility could accommodate a range of restaurant types, thus reinforcing the landlord’s rentability of the space in the foreseeable future.

### We design.

This mixed-use project includes three 2,000 sf single-family housing units, each with three stories with a basement, and a 5,842 sf full-service restaurant and bar with first and second floor and rooftop deck seating. Both the residential and restaurant require new, full MEP systems. The exterior requires envelope and structural restoration as a result of previous fires and will also feature exterior lighting to showcase the restored building at night. Site master planning for parking for residents, restaurant patrons and food service deliveries will be handled with the assistance of the civil engineer. In addition to the existing building rehabilitation, new construction will support kitchen and food service areas of the restaurant, such as coolers, dish washing and keg storage. Full interior design and kitchen design also includes a 5,000 pound masonry pizza oven.

![Northern Gateway Renderings York, PA](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/2019-3D-View-EXTERIOR-PERSPECTIVE-VIEW-1.jpg)

![Northern Gateway Renderings York, PA](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/2019-3D-View-INTERIOR-PERSPECTIVE-VIEW-1.jpg)

### We deliver.

The project will result in the perfect combination of exterior historic restoration blended with modern new construction and integration of modern amenities for an industrial fit and finish. A painted mural on the side of the building will proudly communicate the location as the “Gateway to the City”.

## Single Family Lots

## Notable Projects

Church Road - New Construction

Warehaus completed the site planning and development for Lots 2, 3, and 4 along Church Road in Manchester Township, York County, Pennsylvania. The scope of services includes several key tasks. A comprehensive survey was conducted, resulting in an Existing Conditions Plan. Geotechnical investigation determined soil suitability for stormwater infiltration, influencing the Stormwater Management Plan required by township ordinance. An Erosion and Sediment Control Plan was prepared and submitted to the York County Conservation District. The Stormwater Management incorporated the site layout, grading, and erosion control details. Additionally, construction stakeout was coordinated, and a post-construction As-built Plan was prepared to satisfy municipal and county records. A Driveway Highway Occupancy Permit (HOP) was secured, and a Site Development Plan was created to support the building permit application, ensuring compliance with zoning and impervious cover limits.

## Test Fit Capabilities

Our test fit services provide a preliminary floor plan to assess if a space can meet your specific needs. This quick and cost-effective process helps confirm room capacities, layout efficiencies, and operational requirements, guiding you toward well thought-out space plans. By ensuring all components fit cohesively and maintaining essential adjacencies, our test fits serve as a solid foundation for the schematic design phase, allowing you to make informed decisions with confidence.

## Notable Projects

Office

_14 West Market Street – York, PA_

Modification an existing office space to accommodate a reduced footprint and a flexible remote work environment. The team designed workspaces for 16 employees, adjusting meeting space sizes, and creating supporting spaces for a hybrid in-person and remote setup, in an effort to determine whether the client could remain at the current location with these modifications or if they needed to seek a new office space.

_Paypal Toronto – Toronto, Canada_

_2019 AIA Central Pennsylvania Excellence in Design Award Winner_

Investigation and selection of a new workspace for PayPal flagship office. Performed workspace analysis and developed test fit-outs to narrow the selection process. Different work type spaces – open, semi-private and private booths – were provided to acknowledge diversity in workstyles.

_320 Poplar Corporate Office – Camp Hill, PA_

Design and calculation of the layout for a potential office space in an existing building under new ownership. The design includes a reception area, three individual office spaces, a bullpen area for three people, and additional cubicle space if feasible.

Medical

_Queensgate Practice – York, PA_

Due Diligence Study for Wellspan acquisitions – Test Fit of 2 floors, structural planning for removal of existing partial mezzanines and construction of new mezzanines for the entire floor plate, utility and site master planning. Close care was taken to presenting a vision for the exterior modification to provide a new façade to the building to purposefully transform the building use and function from its original intent to ensure ease of patient flow, function, parking and wayfinding.

_540 South George Street – York, PA_

Due Diligence Study for Wellspan acquisitions – Test Fit of 2 floors, structural confirmations of loads to be applied to the existing floor systems, utility modifications master planning. Special intention was given to maintaining as much, if not all, of the historic structure and integrity. This study involved placement of two practices into the space, Family Medicine and a Dental Practice.

_Wyomissing Building B – Wyomissing, PA_

Test Fit designs for the Wyomissing B and E buildings, including areas for nephrology and sleep apnea treatment. The spaces included restrooms, exam rooms, offices, storage, laundry, waiting room, kitchen, front desk and tech space.

Hospitality

_Ballpark Commons – York, PA_

Test Fit designs for a first-floor restaurant in a high-profile, community-focused location

## Providing site services to make way for a state-of-the-art healthcare center

In the Summer of 2019, WellSpan broke ground on its new, state-of-the-art Heart & Vascular Center, adjacent to the Apple Hill Health Campus in York Township. Warehaus provided civil services including site design and permitting with York Township, NPDES permitting and Erosion and Sedimentation Control Plan approvals with the Pennsylvania Department of Environmental Protection and York County Conservation District, and traffic design and permitting of a Highway Occupancy Permit with PennDOT. Each aspect of the project provided unique challenges.



Project Details

Client

WellSpan Health

Size

Undisclosed

Completion Date

2021

Services

[Civil](https://warehausae.com/service/civil/)

Industries

[Healthcare](https://warehausae.com/industry/healthcare/)

![screen-shot-2020-03-10-at-3-15-57-pm](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/Screen-Shot-2020-03-10-at-3.15.57-PM.webp)

### We listen.

Warehaus was tasked with designing a layout for the Heart and Vascular site that would provide connectivity to the existing Apple Hill Campus. This required adjustments to Monument Road and having York Township abandon its right of way for the existing roadway. This also resulted in a need for a new access point into the Apple Hill Campus off Grantley Road. Warehaus also had to ensure adequate parking was provided for the needs of the Heart and Vascular Center, including additional spaces for the parking-constrained campus. Furthermore, several lots owned by the Client were consolidated into two lots via a reverse subdivision plan. Each of these aspects required close coordination with the Township throughout the Subdivision and Land Development Plan permitting process.



### We design.

Warehaus faced challenges with the NPDES permitting, as the site is not favorable for infiltrating stormwater generated by the new development. In its history, the site was also an orchard. Warehaus prepared watershed studies to satisfy the PA DEP’s concerns that the site would contribute to the degradation of the watershed. Warehaus also coordinated with a geotechnical consultant to prove that the site’s soils were safe for development. Ultimately approvals were obtained.

![wellspan-heart-and-vascular_09052024_0002](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/Wellspan-Heart-and-Vascular_09052024_0002.webp)

![wellspan-heart-and-vascular_09052024_0000](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/Wellspan-Heart-and-Vascular_09052024_0000.webp)

### We deliver.

Warehaus prepared a significant traffic design plan that saw the addition of a traffic signal, turn lanes, and road widening. This challenging task was compounded by the need for extensive communications with neighbors by the Client to accommodate such improvements.

## Historic Charm Meets Modern Comforts

In Pennsylvania’s state capital, the 205 State Street Apartments project transforms a historic Harrisburg townhouse into an exceptional housing solution for visiting elected officials. This initiative seeks to seamlessly blend historical charm with modern comfort.

Project Details

Client

Fernandez Realty Group

Size

3,400 sf

Completion Date

April 2024

Services

[Architecture](https://warehausae.com/service/architecture/)

Industries

[Adaptive Reuse](https://warehausae.com/industry/adaptive-reuse/), [Historic](https://warehausae.com/industry/historic/), [Multi-Family](https://warehausae.com/industry/multi-family/)

![205 State Street Apartments in Harrisburg, Pennsylvania](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/07/state-street-apartments-06192024-5-768x400.webp)

### We listen.

Warehaus, in collaboration with structural engineering firm Kenneth B. Robinson Associates, approached the project by first providing detailed existing conditions modeling.

### We design.

Warehaus also managed concept design, construction documentation, permitting, and bidding phases with a focus on living space design that merge aesthetics with functionality.

![state-street-apartments_06192024-10](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/07/state-street-apartments-06192024-10-768x400.webp)

![state-street-apartments_06192024-15](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/07/state-street-apartments-06192024-15-768x400.webp)

### We deliver.

The resulting apartments, designed as higher-end one-bedroom units, will be curated to include a well-appointed kitchen, a comfortable living area, and a dedicated laundry space complete with washer and dryer. This modern offering is seamlessly integrated within the historical framework of a townhouse constructed in the late 19th century.
