# Gratz Bank Tower Repair

## Minersville, PA

![screenshot-2024-09-17-083457](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/Screenshot-2024-09-17-083457-700x688.webp)

![default](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/DJI_0029-700x700.webp)

![2023_12_05t16_23_26](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/2023_12_05T16_23_26-700x700.webp)

![2023_12_05t16_19_04](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/2023_12_05T16_19_04-700x700.webp)

![screenshot-2024-09-17-083457](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/Screenshot-2024-09-17-083457-700x688.webp)

![default](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/DJI_0029-700x700.webp)

![2023_12_05t16_23_26](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/2023_12_05T16_23_26-700x700.webp)

![2023_12_05t16_19_04](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/2023_12_05T16_19_04-700x700.webp)

![screenshot-2024-09-17-083457](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/Screenshot-2024-09-17-083457-700x688.webp)

![default](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/DJI_0029-700x700.webp)

![2023_12_05t16_23_26](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/2023_12_05T16_23_26-700x700.webp)

![2023_12_05t16_19_04](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/2023_12_05T16_19_04-700x700.webp)

## Repair and Preservation Efforts for Lightning-Damaged Tower

Warehaus was tasked with managing the repair and restoration efforts for the historic tower at Gratz Bank in Minersville, PA, following damage caused by a recent lightning strike. The coordination efforts involved multiple stages to stabilize the immediate damage and plan for permanent repairs in compliance with historic preservation requirements.


Project Details

Client

LINKBANK

Size

N/A

Completion Date

2023

Services

[Architecture](https://warehausae.com/service/architecture/), [Structural](https://warehausae.com/service/structural/)

Industries

[Building Sciences](https://warehausae.com/industry/building-sciences/)

![screenshot-2024-09-17-083457](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/Screenshot-2024-09-17-083457.webp)

### We listen.

Initially, Warehaus conducted comprehensive field work to document the extent of the damage. This included assessing the dislodged stone masonry units and shifted lintels at the southeastern corner of the tower. Notably, two stone masonry units had fallen to the street, and both eastern and southern stone lintels had shifted outward, impacting the existing copper roof and interior wood structural elements. Additional damage to brick masonry, stucco, and windows was also documented around the tower.

### We design.

Following the damage assessment, Warehaus prioritized stabilization measures to prevent further deterioration. They proposed various options for repair, each addressing the specific challenges presented by the lightning strike. These options ranged from repairing and replacing masonry in-kind while stabilizing the copper roof and wood structure, to more extensive approaches involving the removal of existing components and installation of a new flat roof.


![2023_12_05t16_19_04](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/2023_12_05T16_19_04.webp)

![2023_12_05t16_23_26](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/2023_12_05T16_23_26.webp)

### We deliver.

Throughout the project, Warehaus maintained close coordination with local authorities and HARB to ensure all necessary permits and clearances were obtained for the repair and restoration work. This involved preparing and submitting documentation to support the building permit applications, detailing the planned repairs and restoration efforts to restore the tower to its historic integrity.

# 800 South Queen Street Multi-Family Apartments

## Lancaster, PA

![Rendering of the future multi-family apartments at 800 South Queen Street in Lancaster, PA](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/800-S-Queen-view-BRANDED-700x700.jpg)

![Groundbreaking for the future multi-family apartments at 800 South Queen Street in Lancaster, PA](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/800-South-Queen-Street-Groundbreaking_080323_20230803_48-700x700.webp)

![Rendering of the future multi-family apartments at 800 South Queen Street in Lancaster, PA](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/800-S-Queen-view-BRANDED-700x700.jpg)

![Groundbreaking for the future multi-family apartments at 800 South Queen Street in Lancaster, PA](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/800-South-Queen-Street-Groundbreaking_080323_20230803_48-700x700.webp)

![Rendering of the future multi-family apartments at 800 South Queen Street in Lancaster, PA](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/800-S-Queen-view-BRANDED-700x700.jpg)

![Groundbreaking for the future multi-family apartments at 800 South Queen Street in Lancaster, PA](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/800-South-Queen-Street-Groundbreaking_080323_20230803_48-700x700.webp)

## Transforming Lancaster's South Queen Street

Recent studies show a typical employee working 40 hours a week is unable to afford rent for a two-bedroom apartment anywhere in the nation. The U.S. has faced a major rental housing crisis, and Lancaster is similarly presented with an urgent challenge when it comes to rental opportunities for working families. One solution to the rental housing crisis in Lancaster is the OZFund, Inc., a property developer with a community focus and a mission to transform distressed neighborhoods in Central Pennsylvania into thriving economic

Project Details

Client

Queen Street Development Partners

Size

52 rental units

Completion Date

In Construction

Services

[Architecture](https://warehausae.com/service/architecture/)

Industries

[Multi-Family](https://warehausae.com/industry/multi-family/)

![800-south-queen-street-groundbreaking_080323_20230803_59](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/800-South-Queen-Street-Groundbreaking_080323_20230803_59-768x400.jpg)

### We listen.

The first OZFund project is a large property located at 800 South Queen Street, formerly Rebman’s warehouse. To help design the project, OZ Fund selected Warehaus as their design partner. By working side by side, Warehaus complements OZFund’s expertise to create a well-crafted solution built on collective insights and collaboration. Harrisburg-based Steelworks Construction was selected as the project manager and general contractor.

### We design.

800 South Queen will be a four-story mixed-use building that includes Rebmans’ Fresh Market, a convenience/grocery store on the first floor that will feature fresh, locally sourced foods prepackaged for convenience. The remainder of the building houses 52 workforce family rental units—a mix of studio, ones and two-bedroom units. Each apartment offers a built-in washer/dryer, Wi-Fi, parking, and security. Ample onsite parking is available for both tenants and customers visiting the grocery store, as well as bicycle storage and easy pedestrian access. The property’s environmental focus is best showcased in the vegetative green roof, featuring raised beds for growing fresh vegetables and flowers as well as serving as a treatment for stormwater runoff.

![800-s-queen-view-branded](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/800-S-Queen-view-BRANDED-1-768x400.jpg)

![moneyshot-queen-street](https://warehausae.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/MONEYSHOT-QUEEN-STREET-768x400.jpg)

### We deliver.

Affordable housing improves the well-being of families by freeing up funds for essential needs, while also benefiting communities through economic vitality, employee retention, and opportunities for individuals to enhance their economic situation and community involvement. Groundbreaking for 800 South Queen Street occurred in August 2023.

## Test Fit Capabilities

Our test fit services provide a preliminary floor plan to assess if a space can meet your specific needs. This quick and cost-effective process helps confirm room capacities, layout efficiencies, and operational requirements, guiding you toward well thought-out space plans. By ensuring all components fit cohesively and maintaining essential adjacencies, our test fits serve as a solid foundation for the schematic design phase, allowing you to make informed decisions with confidence.

## Notable Projects

Office

_14 West Market Street – York, PA_

Modification an existing office space to accommodate a reduced footprint and a flexible remote work environment. The team designed workspaces for 16 employees, adjusting meeting space sizes, and creating supporting spaces for a hybrid in-person and remote setup, in an effort to determine whether the client could remain at the current location with these modifications or if they needed to seek a new office space.

_Paypal Toronto – Toronto, Canada_

_2019 AIA Central Pennsylvania Excellence in Design Award Winner_

Investigation and selection of a new workspace for PayPal flagship office. Performed workspace analysis and developed test fit-outs to narrow the selection process. Different work type spaces – open, semi-private and private booths – were provided to acknowledge diversity in workstyles.

_320 Poplar Corporate Office – Camp Hill, PA_

Design and calculation of the layout for a potential office space in an existing building under new ownership. The design includes a reception area, three individual office spaces, a bullpen area for three people, and additional cubicle space if feasible.

Medical

_Queensgate Practice – York, PA_

Due Diligence Study for Wellspan acquisitions – Test Fit of 2 floors, structural planning for removal of existing partial mezzanines and construction of new mezzanines for the entire floor plate, utility and site master planning. Close care was taken to presenting a vision for the exterior modification to provide a new façade to the building to purposefully transform the building use and function from its original intent to ensure ease of patient flow, function, parking and wayfinding.

_540 South George Street – York, PA_

Due Diligence Study for Wellspan acquisitions – Test Fit of 2 floors, structural confirmations of loads to be applied to the existing floor systems, utility modifications master planning. Special intention was given to maintaining as much, if not all, of the historic structure and integrity. This study involved placement of two practices into the space, Family Medicine and a Dental Practice.

_Wyomissing Building B – Wyomissing, PA_

Test Fit designs for the Wyomissing B and E buildings, including areas for nephrology and sleep apnea treatment. The spaces included restrooms, exam rooms, offices, storage, laundry, waiting room, kitchen, front desk and tech space.

Hospitality

_Ballpark Commons – York, PA_

Test Fit designs for a first-floor restaurant in a high-profile, community-focused location

## Test Fit Capabilities

Our test fit services provide a preliminary floor plan to assess if a space can meet your specific needs. This quick and cost-effective process helps confirm room capacities, layout efficiencies, and operational requirements, guiding you toward well thought-out space plans. By ensuring all components fit cohesively and maintaining essential adjacencies, our test fits serve as a solid foundation for the schematic design phase, allowing you to make informed decisions with confidence.

## Test Fit Capabilities

Our test fit services provide a preliminary floor plan to assess if a space can meet your specific needs. This quick and cost-effective process helps confirm room capacities, layout efficiencies, and operational requirements, guiding you toward well thought-out space plans. By ensuring all components fit cohesively and maintaining essential adjacencies, our test fits serve as a solid foundation for the schematic design phase, allowing you to make informed decisions with confidence.

## Notable Projects

Office

_14 West Market Street – York, PA_

Modification an existing office space to accommodate a reduced footprint and a flexible remote work environment. The team designed workspaces for 16 employees, adjusting meeting space sizes, and creating supporting spaces for a hybrid in-person and remote setup, in an effort to determine whether the client could remain at the current location with these modifications or if they needed to seek a new office space.

_Paypal Toronto – Toronto, Canada_

_2019 AIA Central Pennsylvania Excellence in Design Award Winner_

Investigation and selection of a new workspace for PayPal flagship office. Performed workspace analysis and developed test fit-outs to narrow the selection process. Different work type spaces – open, semi-private and private booths – were provided to acknowledge diversity in workstyles.

_320 Poplar Corporate Office – Camp Hill, PA_

Design and calculation of the layout for a potential office space in an existing building under new ownership. The design includes a reception area, three individual office spaces, a bullpen area for three people, and additional cubicle space if feasible.

Medical

_Queensgate Practice – York, PA_

Due Diligence Study for Wellspan acquisitions – Test Fit of 2 floors, structural planning for removal of existing partial mezzanines and construction of new mezzanines for the entire floor plate, utility and site master planning. Close care was taken to presenting a vision for the exterior modification to provide a new façade to the building to purposefully transform the building use and function from its original intent to ensure ease of patient flow, function, parking and wayfinding.

_540 South George Street – York, PA_

Due Diligence Study for Wellspan acquisitions – Test Fit of 2 floors, structural confirmations of loads to be applied to the existing floor systems, utility modifications master planning. Special intention was given to maintaining as much, if not all, of the historic structure and integrity. This study involved placement of two practices into the space, Family Medicine and a Dental Practice.

_Wyomissing Building B – Wyomissing, PA_

Test Fit designs for the Wyomissing B and E buildings, including areas for nephrology and sleep apnea treatment. The spaces included restrooms, exam rooms, offices, storage, laundry, waiting room, kitchen, front desk and tech space.

Hospitality

_Ballpark Commons – York, PA_

Test Fit designs for a first-floor restaurant in a high-profile, community-focused location
